meta.description: Catalogue local des objets synchronisé depuis PokéAPI.

entries.name: Ability Capsule
entries.shortEffectText: Switches a Pokémon between its two possible (non-Hidden) Abilities.
entries.effectText: Switches a Pokémon between its two possible (non-Hidden) Abilities.
entries.flavorText:
A capsule that allows a Pokémon with two Abilities
to switch between these Abilities when it is used.

entries.name: Ability Patch
entries.flavorText:
A patch that allows a Pokémon with a regular Ability
to have a rare Ability.

entries.name: Ability Urge
entries.shortEffectText: Forcibly activates a friendly Pokémon’s ability.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Selects another friendly Pokémon at random.  If that Pokémon’s ability is normally activated by some condition—i.e., is not continuous and passive—its effect is forcibly activated.

This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
When used, it activates the Ability of an
ally Pokémon.

entries.name: Abomasite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Abomasnow to Mega Evolve into Mega Abomasnow.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Abomasnow to Mega Evolve into Mega Abomasnow.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Abomasnow hold it, and this stone will enable
it to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Abra Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Absolite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Absol to Mega Evolve into Mega Absol.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Absol to Mega Evolve into Mega Absol.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Absol hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Absorb Bulb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Raises the holder’s Special Attack by one stage when it takes Water-type damage.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder takes Water-type damage from a move, its Special Attack rises by one stage and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Atk
if the holder is hit with a Water-type attack. It can only
be used once.

entries.name: Acro Bike
entries.shortEffectText: More maneuverable than the Mach Bike, and allows hopping along rails.
entries.effectText: Increases movement speed outside or in caves.  Slower than the Mach Bike.  Can perform various tricks, allowing the trainer to reach certain special areas.
entries.flavorText:
This folding bike allows you to perform actions
such as wheelies and bunny hops.

entries.name: Adamant Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Attack will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Atk will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Adamant Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Boosts the damage from Dialga’s Dragon-type and Steel-type moves by 20%.
entries.effectText:
Held by Dialga
:   Holder’s Dragon- and Steel-type moves have 1.2× their usual power.
entries.flavorText:
A brightly gleaming orb to be held by Dialga.
It boosts the power of Dragon- and Steel-type
moves when it is held.

entries.name: Adrenaline Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Makes wild Pokémon more likely to summon allies.  Held: increases the holder’s Speed by one stage when affected by Intimidate.
entries.effectText: Makes wild Pokémon more likely to summon allies.  Held: increases the holder’s Speed by one stage when affected by Intimidate.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it boosts Speed
when intimidated.

entries.name: Adventure Guide
entries.shortEffectText: Contains basic gameplay information.
entries.effectText: Contains basic gameplay information.
entries.flavorText:
A compact device that automatically collects and
records advice when a Trainer is out on an adventure.

entries.name: Aerodactyl Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Aerodactylite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Aerodactyl to Mega Evolve into Mega Aerodactyl.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Aerodactyl to Mega Evolve into Mega Aerodactyl.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Aerodactyl hold it, and this stone will enable
it to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Aggronite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Aggron to Mega Evolve into Mega Aggron.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Aggron to Mega Evolve into Mega Aggron.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Aggron hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Aguav Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike bitter flavor.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP.  If the holder dislikes bitter flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Special Defense), it will also become confused.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a
pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates
the taste.

entries.name: Air Balloon
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Grants immunity to Ground-type moves, Spikes, and Toxic Spikes. Consumed when the holder takes damage from a move.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   Holder is immune to Ground-type moves, Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Arena Trap.

    This effect does not apply during Gravity or Ingrain.

    When the holder takes damage from a move, this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder will
float in the air until hit. Once the holder is hit, this item
will burst.

entries.name: Air Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of
colorful letter sets.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Alakazite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Alakazam to Mega Evolve into Mega Alakazam.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Alakazam to Mega Evolve into Mega Alakazam.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Alakazam hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

names.ja-hrkt: ママンボウのねんえき
names.ja: ママンボウのねんえき

entries.name: Aloraichium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for aloraichium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for aloraichium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Alolan Raichu’s Thunderbolt to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Aloraichium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Alola Raichu to upgrade Thunderbolt into Stoked Sparksurfer.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Alola Raichu to upgrade Thunderbolt into Stoked Sparksurfer.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades
Alolan Raichu’s Thunderbolt to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Altarianite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Altaria to Mega Evolve into Mega Altaria.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Altaria to Mega Evolve into Mega Altaria.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Altaria hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Amaze Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Increases the total number of Berries by 2, increases the chance of Berry mutation, and causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
entries.effectText: Increases the total number of Berries by 2, increases the chance of Berry mutation, and causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Ampharosite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Ampharos to Mega Evolve into Mega Ampharos.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Ampharos to Mega Evolve into Mega Ampharos.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Ampharos hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Amulet Coin
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Doubles the money earned from a battle. Does not stack with Luck Incense.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   If the holder participated in a trainer battle, the trainer earns twice the usual prize money.  This effect applies even if the holder fainted.

    This effect does not stack with any other similar effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles any
prize money received if the holding Pokémon joins
a battle.

entries.name: Antidote
entries.shortEffectText: Cures poison.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures poison.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating poisoning. It can be
used to lift the effects of being poisoned from
a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Apicot Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Special Defense.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its Special Defense by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its Sp. Def stat will increase when it’s in
a pinch.

names.ja-hrkt: カジッチュのかじゅう
names.ja: カジッチュのかじゅう

entries.name: Apricorn Box
entries.shortEffectText: Holds Apricorns.
entries.effectText: Stores Apricorns.
entries.flavorText:
A handy box where you can store up to 99 of each
kind of Apricorn.

entries.name: Aqua Suit
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to ride Kyogre in the Cave of Origin.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to ride Kyogre in the Cave of Origin.
entries.flavorText:
A suit made with the collective technological
know-how of Team Aqua. It can withstand
any impact.

entries.name: Armor Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Shieldon.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive a Shieldon.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
on the land. It looks to be from some kind of
protective collar.

entries.name: Armor Pass
entries.flavorText:
A pass to go to the Isle of Armor. Show it to the rail
staff at Wedgehurst Station.

entries.name: Armorite Ore
entries.flavorText:
Rare ore that can be obtained on the Isle of Armor.
Something good will happen if you give it to a collector
on the Isle of Armor.

entries.name: Articuno Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Aspear Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when frozen to cure frozen.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is frozen, it consumes this item to thaw itself.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures freezing.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from being frozen on its
own in battle.

entries.name: Assault Vest
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Special Defense to 1.5×.  Prevents the holder from selecting a status move.
entries.effectText: Raises the holder’s Special Defense to 1.5×.  Prevents the holder from selecting a status move.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This offensive
vest raises Sp. Def but prevents the use of
status moves.

entries.name: Audinite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Audino to Mega Evolve into Mega Audino.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Audino to Mega Evolve into Mega Audino.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Audino hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: AuroraTicket
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Birth Island and Deoxys.
entries.effectText: Provides access to Birth Island and Deoxys.
entries.flavorText:
A ticket required to board the ship
to BIRTH ISLAND.
It glows beautifully.

entries.name: Autograph
entries.flavorText:
The autograph you got from the Vermilion City Gym
Leader, Lt. Surge. He wrote, “Good luck!”

entries.name: Awakening
entries.shortEffectText: Cures sleep.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures sleep.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine to wake the sleeping. It can be used
to rouse a single Pokémon from the clutches of sleep.

entries.name: Azure Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Allows entry into the Hall of Origin. Unreleased.
entries.flavorText:
A flute that puts out echoing sounds that do not
seem to be of this world. No one knows who
made it.

entries.name: Babiri Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Steel-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Steel-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Steel-type attack.

entries.name: Bach’s Food Tin
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bach sells
is curiously popular in Galar.

entries.name: Balm Mushroom
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Hungry Maid for 25000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A rare mushroom that gives off a nice fragrance.
It can be sold at a high price to shops.

entries.name: Band Autograph
entries.flavorText:
It’s signed by all members of the Galar region’s most
charismatic band, the Maximizers.

entries.name: Banettite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Banette to Mega Evolve into Mega Banette.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Banette to Mega Evolve into Mega Banette.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Banette hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

names.ja-hrkt: ドジョッチのねんえき
names.ja: ドジョッチのねんえき

names.ja-hrkt: よこストライプカップ
names.ja: よこストライプカップ

entries.name: Basement Key
entries.shortEffectText: HS: Key to the tunnel under Goldenrod City. Gen III: Key to New Mauville.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer outside of battle
:   Opens the door to the basement tunnel under Goldenrod City.
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens a door in the
Goldenrod Tunnel.

entries.name: Basement Key
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for basement-key--goldenrod
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for basement-key--goldenrod
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens a door in the
Goldenrod Tunnel.

entries.name: Basement Key
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for basement-key--new-mauville
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for basement-key--new-mauville
entries.flavorText:
The key to New Mauville, which was constructed
beneath Mauville City.

entries.name: Beach Glass
entries.flavorText:
A piece of colored glass. Waves have rounded its edges.
It’s slightly rough to the touch.

entries.name: Bead Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL to be held by a
POKéMON. It will bear the print of
the POKéMON holding it.

entries.name: Beast Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 5× for Ultra Beasts and 0.1× for all other Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If the wild Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, this ball has a catch rate of 5×.  Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 0.1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that has a low success
rate for catching a Pokémon.

entries.name: Beedrillite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Beedrill to Mega Evolve into Mega Beedrill.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Beedrill to Mega Evolve into Mega Beedrill.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Beedrill hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Bellsprout Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Belue Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Beauty, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Berry Juice
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 20 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 20 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A 100 percent pure juice made of Berries.
It can be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Berry Pots
entries.shortEffectText: Allows portable berry growing.
entries.effectText: Contains four portable pots of soil suitable for growing berries.
entries.flavorText:
Handy containers for cultivating Berries wherever
you go.

entries.name: Berry Pouch
entries.shortEffectText: Holds berries.
entries.effectText: Stores Berries.
entries.flavorText:
A pouch for carrying BERRIES.
It is attached to the BAG’s
compartment for important items.

entries.name: Berry Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A berry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Bicycle
entries.shortEffectText: Use for fast transit.
entries.effectText: Increases movement speed outside or in caves.  In high gear, allows the trainer to hop over some rocks and ascend muddy slopes.
entries.flavorText:
A folding Bicycle that enables a rider
to get around much faster than a pair
of Running Shoes will allow.

entries.name: Big Malasada
entries.shortEffectText: Cures major status ailments and confusion.
entries.effectText: Cures major status ailments and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
The Alola region’s local specialty—fried bread.
It can be used once to heal all the status conditions
of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Big Mushroom
entries.shortEffectText: Fire Red and Leaf Green: Trade for prior Level-up moves. Sell for 2500 Pokédollars, or to Hungry Maid for 5000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A very large and rare mushroom. It’s popular with
a certain class of collectors.

entries.name: Big Nugget
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Ore Collector for 30000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A big nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous
gleam. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

entries.name: Big Pearl
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 3750 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 7500 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A rather large pearl that has a very nice silvery sheen.
It can be sold at a high price to shops.

entries.name: Big Root
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases HP recovered from draining moves, Ingrain, and Aqua Ring by 3/10 (30%).
entries.effectText: Held: HP restored from Absorb, Aqua Ring, Drain Punch, Dream Eater, Giga Drain, Ingrain, Leech Life, Leech Seed, and Mega Drain is increased by 30%.  Damage inflicted is not affected.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the
amount of HP the holder restores from
HP-stealing moves.

entries.name: Bike
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for bike--green
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for bike--green
entries.flavorText:
A folding bike that enables a rider to get around
much faster than with Running Shoes.

entries.name: Bike
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for bike--yellow
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for bike--yellow
entries.flavorText:
A folding Bike that enables a rider to get around
much faster than with Running Shoes.

entries.name: Bike Voucher
entries.shortEffectText: Trade in Cerulean bike shop for a Bicycle.
entries.effectText: May be traded for a Bicycle.
entries.flavorText:
Take this voucher to the BIKE SHOP
in CERULEAN CITY and exchange it
for a bicycle.

entries.name: Binding Band
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Doubles the per-turn damage of multi-turn trapping moves.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   Moves used by the holder that trap and damage a target for multiple turns (e.g. Bind, Fire Spin) inflict twice their usual per-turn damage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a band that
increases the power of binding moves used by
the holder.

entries.name: Black Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Heavy Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Heavy Ball.
entries.flavorText:
A black Apricorn. It has a scent beyond
one’s experience.

entries.name: Black Belt
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Fighting-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Fighting moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This belt helps
the wearer to focus and boosts the power of
Fighting-type moves.

entries.name: Black Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Halves the wild Pokémon encounter rate.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Decreases the wild Pokémon encounter rate by 50%.
entries.flavorText:
A flute made from black glass. It makes it easier to
encounter strong Pokémon in the place you use it.

entries.name: Black Glasses
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Dark-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Dark moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a pair of
shady-looking glasses that boost the power of
Dark-type moves.

entries.name: Black Mane Hair
entries.flavorText:
Mane hair that belonged to the King of Bountiful
Harvests’ beloved steed, Spectrier. The hair is very
smooth to the touch.

entries.name: Black Sludge
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Poison-type holder recovers 1/16 (6.25%) max HP each turn. Non-Poison-Types take 1/8 (12.5%) max HP damage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder is Poison-type, restores 1/16 max HP at the end of each turn.  Otherwise, damages the holder by 1/16 its max HP at the end of each turn.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It gradually
restores HP to Poison-type Pokémon. It damages
any other type.

entries.name: Blastoisinite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Blastoise to Mega Evolve into Mega Blastoise.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Blastoise to Mega Evolve into Mega Blastoise.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Blastoise hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Blazikenite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Blaziken to Mega Evolve into Mega Blaziken.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Blaziken to Mega Evolve into Mega Blaziken.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Blaziken hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Bloom Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of pretty
floral patterns.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Blue Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Lure Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Lure Ball.
entries.flavorText: A blue Apricorn. It smells a bit like grass.

entries.name: Blue Card
entries.shortEffectText: Keeps track of points from Buena’s show.
entries.effectText: Allows the trainer to answer the daily question on Buena’s radio show.  Records the points earned for correct answers.
entries.flavorText:
A card to save points for the
Buena’s Password show.

entries.name: Blue Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Cures sleep.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures sleep.
entries.flavorText:
A flute made from blue glass. It awakens
sleeping Pokémon.

entries.name: Blue Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Kyogre to the Embedded Tower.
entries.effectText: Causes Kyogre to appear in the Embedded Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A shiny blue orb that is said to have a legend tied
to it. It’s known to have a deep connection with the
Hoenn region.

entries.name: Blue Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for blue-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for blue-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Lana
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Blue Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Beauty while in a contest.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Beauty during a Super Contest’s Visual Competition.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest,
it raises the Beauty aspect of the Pokémon
holding it.

entries.name: Blue Shard
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
entries.effectText:
No effect.

In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a Rain Dance TM in the house midway along the southern section of Route 212.

In Platinum, trade to move tutors on Route 212, in Snowpoint City, and in the Survival Area.  Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Chesto Berry, an Oran Berry, and a Wiki Berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in Violet City.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Bluk Berry, a Cornn Berry, and a Kelpsy Berry with the Juggler near the Pal Park entrance in Fuchsia City.
entries.flavorText:
A small blue shard. It appears to be a fragment of
some sort of implement made long ago.

names.ja-hrkt: あおぞらおはなピック
names.ja: あおぞらおはなピック

entries.name: Bluk Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Beauty. Its blue flesh is dry when eaten.

entries.name: Blunder Policy
entries.flavorText:
Raises Speed sharply when a Pokémon misses with a
move because of accuracy.

entries.name: Bob’s Food Tin
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bob sells
is curiously popular in Galar.

entries.name: Boiled Egg
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Even just one boiled egg can
really enhance a dish.

entries.name: Bold Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Defense will grow
more easily, but its Attack will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Boost Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
entries.effectText: Causes soil to dry out in 4 hours.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Bottle Cap
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Mr. Hyper to maximize one of a Pokémon’s genes.
entries.effectText: Trade to Mr. Hyper to set one of a Pokémon’s genes to 31.
entries.flavorText:
A beautiful bottle cap that gives off a silver gleam.
Some people are happy to receive one.

entries.name: Brave Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Attack will grow
more easily, but its Speed will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Bread
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. An indispensable tool for
mopping up that extra curry left on your plate.

entries.name: Brick Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of a
tough-looking brick pattern.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Bridge Mail D
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring an illustration of a bright-red
drawbridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Bridge Mail M
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring an illustration of a majestically
arched bridge. Have a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Bridge Mail S
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring an illustration of a bridge that
seems to pierce the sky. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Bridge Mail T
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring an illustration of a high-tension
steel suspension bridge. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Bridge Mail V
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring an illustration of a
vintage-looking brick bridge. Have a Pokémon hold
it for delivery.

entries.name: Bright Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases the holder’s evasion by 1/9 (11 1/9%).
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   Moves targeting the holder have 0.9× chance to hit.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It casts a tricky
glare that lowers the opposing
Pokémon’s accuracy.

entries.name: Brittle Bones
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The taste boiled off from the
bones is sure to give a dish a light umami taste.

names.ja-hrkt: ブロンズチタンボトル
names.ja: ブロンズチタンボトル

names.ja-hrkt: ブロンズチタンカップ
names.ja: ブロンズチタンカップ

entries.name: Bubble Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of a blue
world underwater.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Bug Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Bug-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Bug-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an insect-like essence. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Bug-type move
one time.

entries.name: Bug Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Bug form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Bug.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Bug form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Bug.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Bug-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Buginium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for buginium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for buginium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Bug-type moves to Bug-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Buginium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Bug moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Bug moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Bug-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Bulbasaur Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Burn Drive
entries.shortEffectText: Grants Genesect a red, Fire-type Techno Blast.
entries.effectText:
Held by Genesect
:   Holder’s buster is red, and its Techno Blast is Fire-type.
entries.flavorText:
A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes
Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes
Fire type.

entries.name: Burn Heal
entries.shortEffectText: Cures a burn.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures a burn.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating burns. It can be
used to heal a single Pokémon suffering from a burn.

entries.name: Calcium
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Special Attack effort and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases Special Attack effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed,
it raises the Sp. Atk base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Calm Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Def will grow
more easily, but its Attack will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Cameruptite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Camerupt to Mega Evolve into Mega Camerupt.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Camerupt to Mega Evolve into Mega Camerupt.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Camerupt hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Camping Gear
entries.flavorText:
Everything you need to set up a tent and get cooking in
a Wild Area or at a camping spot.

entries.name: Carbos
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Speed effort and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases Speed effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed,
it raises the Speed base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Card Key
entries.shortEffectText: HS: Opens doors in the Radio Tower. Gen III: Unlocks Silph Co Doors.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer outside of battle
:   Opens doors in the Goldenrod City Radio Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A card key that opens a shutter in the
Radio Tower.

entries.name: Card Key
entries.flavorText:
A card key that unlocks doors in Silph Co.’s head
office in Saffron City.

entries.name: Careful Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Def will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Atk will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Carrot Seeds
entries.flavorText:
Seeds to plant in a field. The kind of carrot you’ll get is
determined by which field you plant the seeds in.

entries.name: Casteliacone
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
Castelia City’s specialty, soft-serve ice cream.
It can be used once to heal all the status conditions
of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Catching Charm
entries.flavorText:
Holding it is said to increase the chance of getting a
critical catch. Curiously, the charm doesn’t shake much.

entries.name: Caterpie Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Cell Battery
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Raises the holder’s Attack by one stage when it takes Electric-type damage.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder takes Electric-type damage from a move, its Attack rises by one stage and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack
if the holder is hit with an Electric-type attack. It can
only be used once.

entries.name: Chalky Stone
entries.flavorText: A small whitish stone picked up at the edge of the road.

entries.name: Chansey Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Charcoal
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Fire-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Fire moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a combustible
fuel that boosts the power of Fire-type moves.

entries.name: Charizardite X
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Charizard to Mega Evolve into Mega Charizard X.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Charizard to Mega Evolve into Mega Charizard X.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Charizard hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Charizardite Y
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Charizard to Mega Evolve into Mega Charizard Y.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Charizard to Mega Evolve into Mega Charizard Y.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Charizard hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Charmander Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Charti Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Rock-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Rock-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Rock-type attack.

entries.name: Cheri Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when paralyzed to cure paralysis.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is paralyzed, it consumes this item to cure the paralysis.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures paralysis.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from paralysis on its own
in battle.

entries.name: Cherish Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in
order to commemorate a special occasion of
some sort.

entries.name: Chesto Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when asleep to cure sleep.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is asleep, it consumes this item to wake up.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures sleep.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from sleep on its own
in battle.

entries.name: Chilan Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a Normal-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take Normal-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one Normal-type attack.

entries.name: Chill Drive
entries.shortEffectText: Grants Genesect a white, Ice-type Techno Blast.
entries.effectText:
Held by Genesect
:   Holder’s buster is white, and its Techno Blast becomes Ice-type.
entries.flavorText:
A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes
Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes
Ice type.

entries.name: Chipped Pot
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It may be chipped, but tea poured
from it is delicious.

entries.name: Choice Band
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases Attack by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   Holder has 1.5× its Attack.  When the holder attempts to use a move, all its other moves are disabled until it leaves battle or loses this item.

    The restriction ends even if this item is swapped for another Choice item
via Trick or Switcheroo.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This curious
headband boosts Attack but only allows the use of
one move.

entries.name: Choice Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases Speed by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the holder’s Speed by 50%, but restricts it to the first move it uses until it leaves battle or loses this item.  If this item is swapped for another Choice item via Trick or Switcheroo, the holder’s restriction is still lifted, but it will again be restricted to the next move it uses.
(Quirk: If the holder is switched in by U-Turn and it also knows U-Turn, U-Turn becomes its restricted move.)
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This curious
scarf boosts Speed but only allows the use of
one move.

entries.name: Choice Specs
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases Special Attack by 50%, but restricts the holder to only one move.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the holder’s Special Attack by 50%, but restricts it to the first move it uses until it leaves battle or loses this item.  If this item is swapped for another Choice item via Trick or Switcheroo, the holder’s restriction is still lifted, but it will again be restricted to the next move it uses.
(Quirk: If the holder is switched in by U-Turn and it also knows U-Turn, U-Turn becomes its restricted move.)
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. These curious
glasses boost Sp. Atk but only allow the use of
one move.

entries.name: Chople Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Fighting-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Fighting-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Fighting-type attack.

entries.name: Claw Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into an Anorith.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive an Anorith.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sea. It appears to be a fragment of a claw.

entries.name: Cleanse Tag
entries.shortEffectText: Prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Held by lead Pokémon: Prevents wild battles with Pokémon that are lower level than the holder.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep
wild Pokémon away if the holder is the head of
the party.

entries.name: Clear Bell
entries.shortEffectText: HS: Allows Kimono-girls to summon Ho-oh. C: Summons Suicune to the Tin Tower.
entries.effectText: May be given to the Kimono Girls to summon Ho-Oh to the top of the Bell Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A very old-fashioned bell that makes a gentle
ringing sound.

entries.name: Clefairy Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Clever Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases Special Defense effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s Special Defense effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the Sp. Def base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Clothing Trunk
entries.flavorText:
A very light trunk that can carry clothing for you
and your Pokémon.

entries.name: Clover Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A clover-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Coba Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Flying-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Flying-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Flying-type attack.

entries.name: Coconut Milk
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The refined sweetness of this
coconut milk makes it quite popular.

entries.name: Coin Case
entries.shortEffectText: Holds coins for the Game Corner.
entries.effectText: Contains the Coins used by the Game Corner, to a maximum of 50,000.
entries.flavorText:
A case for holding coins obtained at the
Game Corner. It can hold up to 50,000 coins.

entries.name: Colbur Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Dark-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Dark-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Dark-type attack.

entries.name: Colress Machine
entries.shortEffectText: Wakes up the Crustle blocking the way in Seaside Cave.
entries.flavorText:
A special device that wrings out the potential of
Pokémon. It’s an imperfect prototype.

entries.name: Comet Shard
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Ore Collector for 60000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A shard that fell to the ground when a comet
approached. It can be sold at a high price
to shops.

entries.name: Common Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Unknown.
entries.effectText: Unknown.
entries.flavorText:
A perfectly normal stone that might appear to be
valuable to some. It’s all in the eye of the beholder.

entries.name: Contest Costume
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for contest-costume--dress
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for contest-costume--dress
entries.flavorText:
A very cute dress to be worn during the
Contest Spectacular.

entries.name: Contest Costume
entries.shortEffectText: Worn during Pokémon Contests.
entries.effectText: Worn during Pokémon Contests.
entries.flavorText:
A very cool suit to be worn during the
Contest Spectacular.

entries.name: Contest Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Allows participation in Pokémon Contests.
entries.effectText: Allows the trainer to enter Contests.
entries.flavorText:
A pass required for entering Pokémon Contests.
It has a drawing of an award ribbon on its front.

entries.name: Cornn Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Beauty, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Coupon 1
entries.shortEffectText: The first of three tickets used to obtain a Pokétch.
entries.effectText: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.
entries.flavorText:
A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch—
Pokétch for short. Three coupons are needed.

entries.name: Coupon 2
entries.shortEffectText: The second of three tickets used to obtain a Pokétch.
entries.effectText: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.
entries.flavorText:
A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch—
Pokétch for short. Three coupons are needed.

entries.name: Coupon 3
entries.shortEffectText: The last of three tickets used to obtain a Pokétch.
entries.effectText: One of three coupons needed to receive a Pokétch.
entries.flavorText:
A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch—
Pokétch for short. Three coupons are needed.

entries.name: Courage Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: Courage Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: Courage Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: Cover Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Tirtouga.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in a museum to receive a Tirtouga.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sea. It appears as though it could be part of
its back.

entries.name: Cracked Pot
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It may be cracked, but tea poured
from it is delicious.

entries.name: Crown Pass
entries.flavorText:
A pass to go to the Crown Tundra. Show it to the rail
staff at Wedgehurst Station.

entries.name: Cubone Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Custap Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP when using a move to go first.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item.  On the following turn, the holder will act first among moves with the same priority, regardless of Speed.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it will be able to move first just once
when it’s in a pinch.

entries.name: Damp Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Growing time of berries is increased, but the soil dries out slower.
entries.effectText:
Used on a patch of soil
:   Plant’s growth stages will each last 25% more time.  Dries soil out more slowly.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Damp Rock
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Rain Dance by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder’s Rain Dance will create rain lasting for eight turns rather than five.  As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of the move Rain Dance when used by
the holder.

entries.name: Dark Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Dark-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Dark-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of darkness. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Dark-type move
one time.

entries.name: Dark Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Dark form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Dark.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Dark form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Dark.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Dark-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Dark Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Zekrom for the final battle against N.
entries.effectText: Summons Zekrom for the final battle against N.
entries.flavorText:
Zekrom’s body was destroyed and changed into
this stone. It is said to be waiting for the
emergence of a hero.

entries.name: Darkinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for darkinium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for darkinium-z--bag

It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade

entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Dark moves.

This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Dark-type moves to Z-Moves.

individual prizes.

entries.effectText: Records the number of times the trainer has instructed Pokémon in the Pokéathlon.
entries.flavorText:
It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows

entries.effectText: Records the total time spent in the Pokéathlon.
entries.flavorText:
It holds Pokéathlon records. It is a card that shows

the Pokéathlon.

entries.name: Dawn Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves a male Kirlia into Gallade or a female Snorunt into Froslass.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves a male Kirlia into Gallade or a female Snorunt into Froslass.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It sparkles like a glittering eye.

entries.name: Decidium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for decidium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for decidium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Decidueye’s Spirit Shackle to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Decidium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Decidueye to upgrade Spirit Shackle into Sinister Arrow Raid.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Decidueye to upgrade Spirit Shackle into Sinister Arrow Raid.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Decidueye’s Spirit Shackle to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Deep Sea Scale
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles Clamperl’s Special Defense. Traded on a Clamperl: Holder evolves into Gorebyss.
entries.effectText: Held by Clamperl: Doubles the holder’s Special Defense.  Evolves the holder into Gorebyss when traded.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Clamperl. This scale shines
with a faint pink and raises the holder’s
Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: Deep Sea Tooth
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles Clamperl’s Special Attack. Traded on a Clamperl: Holder evolves into Huntail.
entries.effectText: Held by Clamperl: Doubles the holder’s Special Attack.  Evolves the holder into Huntail when traded.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Clamperl. This fang gleams a
sharp silver and raises the holder’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: Destiny Knot
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Infatuates opposing Pokémon when holder is inflicted with infatuation.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder becomes Attracted, the Pokémon it is Attracted to becomes Attracted back.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder
becomes infatuated, the opposing Pokémon will
be, too.

entries.name: Devon Goods
entries.shortEffectText: Contains mechanical parts to be delivered to Captain Stern.
entries.effectText: Contains a machine part to be delivered to Captain Stern.
entries.flavorText:
A package that contains mechanical
parts of some sort made by the
DEVON CORPORATION.

entries.name: Devon Parts
entries.shortEffectText: Allows Captain Stern to set out on his expedition.
entries.effectText: Allows Captain Stern to set out on his expedition.
entries.flavorText:
A case that contains mechanical parts of some sort
made by the Devon Corporation.

entries.name: Devon Scope
entries.shortEffectText: Allows spotting of invisible Kecleon.
entries.effectText: Reveals invisble Kecleon on the overworld.
entries.flavorText:
A special device made by Devon Corporation that
signals the presence of any unseen Pokémon.

entries.name: Devon Scuba Gear
entries.shortEffectText: Worn by the player while underwater.
entries.effectText: Worn by the player while underwater via Dive in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire.
entries.flavorText:
A device made by Devon Corporation that provides
oxygen to users during the use of Dive.

entries.name: Diancite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Diancie to Mega Evolve into Mega Diancie.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Diancie to Mega Evolve into Mega Diancie.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Diancie hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Diglett Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Dire Hit
entries.shortEffectText: Increases the chance of a critical hit in battle.  Raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Increases the target’s critical hit chance by one stage until it leaves the field.  Increases happiness by 1/1/0.
entries.flavorText:
An item that greatly raises the critical-hit ratio of
a Pokémon during a battle. It can be used only once
and wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

entries.name: Dire Hit 2
entries.shortEffectText: Raises critical hit rate by two stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s critical hit rate by two stages.
This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
It can be used many times to raise the critical-hit
ratio of one Pokémon. It wears off if the Pokémon
is withdrawn.

entries.name: Dire Hit 3
entries.shortEffectText: Raises critical hit rate by three stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s critical hit rate by three stages.
This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
It can be used many times to greatly raise a
Pokémon’s critical-hit ratio. It wears off if the
Pokémon is withdrawn.

entries.name: Discount Coupon
entries.shortEffectText: Can be used at a boutique for a 50% discount.  Consumed once used.
entries.effectText: Can be used at a boutique for a 50% discount.  Consumed once used.
entries.flavorText:
This special coupon allows you to buy items at a
discount when you are shopping at a boutique.

entries.name: Ditto Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Dive Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate is 3.5× when underwater, fishing, or surfing.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If the wild Pokémon was encountered by surfing or fishing, this ball has a catch rate of 3.5×.  Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that works
especially well when catching Pokémon that
live underwater.

entries.name: DNA Splicers
entries.shortEffectText: Fuses Kyurem with Reshiram or Zekrom, or splits them apart again.
entries.flavorText:
A splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon.
They are said to have been one in the beginning.

entries.name: DNA Splicers
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for dna-splicers--merge
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for dna-splicers--merge
entries.flavorText:
A splicer that fuses Kyurem and a certain Pokémon.
They are said to have been one in the beginning.

entries.name: DNA Splicers
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for dna-splicers--split
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for dna-splicers--split
entries.flavorText:
A splicer that separates Kyurem and a certain
Pokémon when they have been fused.

entries.name: Doduo Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Dome Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Kabuto.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive a Kabuto.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sea. It could be a shell or carapace.

entries.name: Douse Drive
entries.shortEffectText: Grants Genesect a blue, Water-type Techno Blast.
entries.effectText:
Held by Genesect
:   Holder’s buster is blue, and its Techno Blast is Water-type.
entries.flavorText:
A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes
Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes
Water type.

entries.name: Dowsing Machine
entries.shortEffectText: Use to find hidden items on the field. AKA Itemfinder.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer outside of battle
:   Searches for hidden items.
entries.flavorText:
A high-tech machine that shows the location of
invisible items. It’s attached to the head when
in use.

entries.name: Draco Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Dragon-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Dragon.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Dragon moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Dragon.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Dragon-type moves.

entries.name: Dragon Fang
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Dragon-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Dragon moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This hard and
sharp fang boosts the power of
Dragon-type moves.

entries.name: Dragon Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Dragon-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Dragon-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with a draconic essence. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Dragon-type move
one time.

entries.name: Dragon Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Dragon form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Dragon.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Dragon form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Dragon.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Dragon-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Dragon Scale
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Seadra: Holder evolves into Kingdra.
entries.effectText: Held by Seadra: Evolves the holder into Kingdra when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A very tough and inflexible scale. Dragon-type
Pokémon may be holding this item when caught.

entries.name: Dragon Skull
entries.shortEffectText: Return to the museum in Nacrene City.
entries.effectText: Only used as a plot point; this item is given to the player and taken away in the same cutscene.
entries.flavorText:
A skull of a Pokémon that was said to have braved
the angry waters to fly around the world.

entries.name: Dragonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for dragonium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for dragonium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Dragon-type moves to Dragon-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Dragonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Dragon moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Dragon moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Dragon-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Dratini Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Dread Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Dark-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Dark.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Dark moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Dark.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Dark-type moves.

entries.name: Dream Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Catches Pokémon found in the Dream World.
entries.effectText:
Can only be used in Entree Forest, to catch Pokémon encountered in the Dream World.

Used in battle
:   Catches a wild Pokémon without fail.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes it easier to
catch wild Pokémon while they’re asleep.

entries.name: Dream Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL to be held by a
POKéMON. It will bear the print of
the POKéMON holding it.

entries.name: Dropped Item
entries.shortEffectText: Returned to Curtis or Yancy as part of a sidequest.
entries.flavorText:
The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City
amusement park. It seems it belongs to a boy.

entries.name: Dropped Item
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for dropped-item--red
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for dropped-item--red
entries.flavorText:
The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City
amusement park. It seems it belongs to a boy.

entries.name: Dropped Item
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for dropped-item--yellow
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for dropped-item--yellow
entries.flavorText:
The Xtransceiver found at the Nimbasa City
amusement park. It seems it belongs to a girl.

entries.name: Drowzee Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Dubious Disc
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Porygon2: Holder evolves into Porygon-Z.
entries.effectText: Held by Porygon2: Evolves the holder into Porygon-Z when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A transparent device overflowing with dubious
data. Its producer is unknown.

entries.name: Durin Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cleverness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Dusk Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3.5× at night and in caves.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If it’s currently nighttime or the wild Pokémon was encountered while walking in a cave, this ball has a catch rate of 3.5×.  Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes it
easier to catch wild Pokémon at night or in dark
places such as caves.

entries.name: Dusk Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves a Lampent into Chandelure, a Misdreavus into Mismagius, or a Murkrow into Honchkrow.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves a Lampent into Chandelure, a Misdreavus into Mismagius, or a Murkrow into Honchkrow.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It holds shadows as dark as
can be.

entries.name: Dynamax Band
entries.flavorText:
A Wishing Star has been affixed to it. It lets out a light
that allows Pokémon to Dynamax when at a Power Spot.

entries.name: Dynamax Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will raise the Dynamax Level of a single Pokémon by
one. A higher level means higher HP when Dynamaxed.

entries.flavorText:
