entries.name: Dynite Ore
entries.flavorText:
Mysterious ore that can be obtained in the Max Lair.
Something good will happen if you give it to a
collector in the Crown Tundra.

entries.name: Earth Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ground-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ground.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ground moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Ground.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Ground-type moves.

entries.name: Eevee Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Eevium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for eevium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for eevium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Eevee’s Last Resort to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Eevium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Eevee to upgrade Last Resort into Extreme Evoboost.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Eevee to upgrade Last Resort into Extreme Evoboost.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Eevee’s Last Resort to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Eject Button
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder takes damage from a move, it switches out for a party Pokémon of the Trainer’s choice.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder takes damage directly from a move and does not faint, it switches out for another non-fainted Pokémon in its party, as chosen by the Trainer.
This effect does not activate if another effect prevents the holder from switching out.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder is
hit by an attack, it will be switched out of battle.

entries.name: Eject Pack
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder’s
stats are lowered, it will be switched out of battle.

entries.name: Ekans Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Electabuzz Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Electirizer
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on an Electabuzz: Holder evolves into Electivire.
entries.effectText: Held by Electabuzz: Evolves the holder into Electivire when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A box packed with a tremendous amount of electric
energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Electric Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Electric-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Electric-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of electricity. When held,
it strengthens the power of an Electric-type move
one time.

entries.name: Electric Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Electric form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Electric.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Electric form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Electric.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Electric-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Electric Seed
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed on Electric Terrain and raises the holder’s Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder enters battle during Electric Terrain, or if Electric Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Defense raises by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Defense
on Electric Terrain. It can only be used once.

entries.name: Electrium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for electrium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for electrium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Electric-type moves to Electric-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Electrium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Electric moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Electric moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Electric-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Elevator Key
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks the elevator in Lysandre Labs.
entries.effectText: Unlocks the elevator in Lysandre Labs.
entries.flavorText:
A card key that activates the elevator in Lysandre
Labs. It is emblazoned with Team Flare’s logo.

entries.name: Elixir
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 10 PP for each move.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 10 PP for each move.
entries.flavorText:
This medicine can be used to restore 10 PP to each of
the moves that have been learned by a Pokémon.

entries.name: Endorsement
entries.flavorText:
A letter of recommendation that you’ll need in order to
participate in the Gym Challenge.

entries.name: Energy Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 50 HP, but lowers happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 50 HP.  Decreases happiness by 5/5/10.
entries.flavorText:
A very bitter medicinal powder. It can be used to
restore 60 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Energy Root
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 200 HP, but lowers happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 200 HP.  Decreases happiness by 10/10/15.
entries.flavorText:
An extremely bitter medicinal root. It can be used to
restore 120 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Enigma Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective attack to restore 1/4 max HP.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder takes super-effective damage, it consumes this item to restore 1/4 its max HP.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, being hit by a supereffective attack will
restore its HP.

entries.name: Enigma Stone
entries.shortEffectText: S: Summons Latias H: Summons Latios.
entries.effectText:
When taken to the Pewter City museum, causes Latias or Latios to attack the trainer.

The Pokémon to appear will be whicher can’t be encountered roaming in the wild.
entries.flavorText:
A crystal ball that was excavated from the ground.
It’s a very beautiful stone that is covered with bits
of rock and earth.

entries.name: Enigmatic Card
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks Looker’s motel room on Route 8.
entries.effectText: Unlocks Looker’s motel room on Route 8.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious card.
Written on it is a request for you to go to a
guest room in an Akala motel on Route 8.

entries.name: Eon Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Latias or Latios for a ride.
entries.effectText: Summons Latias or Latios for a ride.
entries.flavorText:
A flute that can be used to summon Latios or Latias
no matter where you are.

entries.name: Eon Ticket
entries.shortEffectText: Provides access to Southern Island and Latias or Latios.
entries.effectText: Provides access to Southern Island and either Latias or Latios, whichever is not available roaming around Hoenn.
entries.flavorText:
The ticket required for sailing on a ferry to the
Southern Island. Does Norman, the Petalburg Gym
Leader, know a secret?!

entries.name: Escape Rope
entries.shortEffectText: Transports user to the outside entrance of a cave.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Transports the trainer to the last-entered dungeon entrance.  Cannot be used outside, in buildings, or in Distortion World, Hall Of Origin, Spear Pillar, or Turnback Cave.
entries.flavorText:
A long and durable rope. Use it to escape instantly
from locations like caves or dungeons. It can be used
any number of times.

entries.name: Ether
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 10 PP for one move.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 10 PP for a selected move.
entries.flavorText:
This medicine can be used to restore 10 PP to a single
selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

entries.name: Everstone
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Prevents level-based evolution from occuring.
entries.effectText: Held: Prevents the holder from evolving naturally.  Evolution initiated by the trainer (Stones, etc) will still work.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. A Pokémon
holding this peculiar stone is prevented
from evolving.

entries.name: Eviolite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder has 1.5× Defense and Special Defense, as long as it’s not fully evolved.
entries.effectText:
Held by a Pokémon that is not fully evolved
:   Holder has 1.5× Defense and Special Defense.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious evolutionary lump. When held by a
Pokémon that can still evolve, it raises both
Defense and Sp. Def.

entries.name: Exeggcute Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Exp. Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will grant a single Pokémon a large amount of
Exp. Points.

entries.name: Exp. Candy M
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will grant a single Pokémon a moderate amount of
Exp. Points.

entries.name: Exp. Candy S
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will grant a single Pokémon a small amount of
Exp. Points.

entries.name: Exp. Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will grant a single Pokémon a very large amount of
Exp. Points.

entries.name: Exp. Candy XS
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it
will grant a single Pokémon a very small amount of
Exp. Points.

entries.name: Exp. Charm
entries.flavorText:
A charm that increases the Exp. Points that Pokémon
can get. A machine-like object is inside the charm.

entries.name: Exp. Share
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Half the experience from a battle is split between Pokémon holding this item.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   Experience is split across two groups: Pokémon who participated in battle, and Pokémon holding this item.  Each Pokémon earns experience as though it had battled alone, divided by the number of Pokémon in its group, then divided by the number of groups. Pokémon holding this item who also participated in battle effectively earn experience twice.

    Fainted Pokémon never earn experience, and empty groups are
ignored; thus, if a single Pokémon is holding this item and the only Pokémon who battled faints from Explosion, the holder will gain full experience.
entries.flavorText:
Turning on this special device will allow all the Pokémon
on your team to receive Exp. Points from battles.

entries.name: Expert Belt
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder’s Super Effective moves do 20% extra damage.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder hits with a super-effective move, its power is raised by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a well-worn
belt that slightly boosts the power of
supereffective moves.

entries.name: Explorer Kit
entries.shortEffectText: Allows visiting the Underground.
entries.effectText: Sends the trainer to the Underground.  Only usable outside.
entries.flavorText:
A bag filled with convenient tools for exploring.
It provides access to the Underground in the
Sinnoh region.

entries.name: Fab Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a
gorgeous, extravagant print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Fairium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for fairium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for fairium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Fairy-type moves to Fairy-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Fairium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fairy moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fairy moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Fairy-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Fairy Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fairy move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fairy move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of the fey. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Fairy-type move
one time.

entries.name: Fairy Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fairy form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fairy.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fairy form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fairy.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Fairy-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

names.ja-hrkt: テラピースフェアリー
names.ja: テラピースフェアリー

entries.name: Fame Checker
entries.shortEffectText: Records information about NPCs.
entries.effectText: Records information on various famous people.
entries.flavorText:
A device that enables you to
recall what you’ve heard and seen
about famous people.

entries.name: Fancy Apple
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. An apple chosen above others.
It has spectacular form and a brilliant sheen.

entries.name: Farfetch’d Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Fashion Case
entries.shortEffectText: Holds Pokémon Accessories for use in Contests.
entries.effectText: Contains Pokémon Accessories.
entries.flavorText:
A lovely case to store colorful Props for your
Pokémon to wear in a musical.

entries.name: Fast Ball
entries.shortEffectText: 4× effectiveness on Pokémon with 100 or greater base speed. (Gen II: Roaming or Fleeing Pokémon). Made from Wht Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

:   If the wild Pokémon’s base speed is 100 or more, its catch rate is 4× normal.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon
that are usually very quick to run away.

entries.name: Favored Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to make it easier to write about
your favorite things. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Festival Ticket
entries.shortEffectText: Hosts a mission in Festival Plaza.
entries.effectText: Hosts a mission in Festival Plaza.
entries.flavorText:
A ticket that allows you to host a mission in
Festival Plaza.

entries.name: Fighting Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fighting-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Fighting-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of combat. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Fighting-type move
one time.

entries.name: Fighting Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fighting form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fighting.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fighting form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fighting.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Fighting-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Fightinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for fightinium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for fightinium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Fighting-type moves to Fighting-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Fightinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fighting moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fighting moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Fighting-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Figy Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike spicy flavor.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP.  If the holder dislikes spicy flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Attack), it will also become confused.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a
pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates
the taste.

names.ja-hrkt: ナミイルカのねんえき
names.ja: ナミイルカのねんえき

entries.name: Fire Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Fire-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Fire-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of fire. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Fire-type move
one time.

entries.name: Fire Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fire form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fire.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Fire form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Fire.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Fire-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Fire Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves an Eevee into Flareon, a Growlithe into Arcanine, a Pansear into Simisear, or a Vulpix into Ninetales.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves an Eevee into Flareon, a Growlithe into Arcanine, a Pansear into Simisear, or a Vulpix into Ninetales.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. The stone has a fiery orange heart.

entries.name: Firium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for firium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for firium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Fire-type moves to Fire-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Firium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fire moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Fire moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Fire-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Fishing Rod
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to fish for Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to fish for Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A fishing rod made by Captain Lana. Cast a line
over piles of underwater rocks to fish for wild
aquatic Pokémon.

entries.name: Fishing Rod
entries.flavorText: Use it by the water to fish up various kinds of Pokémon.

entries.name: Fist Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Fighting-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fighting.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Fighting moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Fighting.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Fighting-type moves.

entries.name: Flame Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of flames
in blazing red.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Flame Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Inflicts Burn on the holder at the end of the turn. Activates after Burn damage would occur.
entries.effectText: Held: Burns the holder at the end of each turn.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a bizarre orb
that will afflict the holder with a burn during battle.

entries.name: Flame Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Fire-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fire.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Fire moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Fire.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Fire-type moves.

entries.name: Float Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder has 0.5× weight.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   Holder has 0.5× weight.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This very light
stone reduces the weight of a Pokémon when held.

entries.name: Flower Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A flower-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Fluffy Tail
entries.shortEffectText: Ends a wild battle.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Ends a wild battle.  Cannot be used in trainer battles.
entries.flavorText:
A toy that attracts the attention of a Pokémon.
It guarantees escape from any battle with
wild Pokémon.

entries.name: Flying Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Flying-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Flying-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of air. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Flying-type move
one time.

entries.name: Flying Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Flying form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Flying.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Flying form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Flying.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Flying-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Flyinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for flyinium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for flyinium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Flying-type moves to Flying-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Flyinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Flying moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Flying moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Flying-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Focus Band
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder has 10% chance to survive attacks or self-inflicted damage at 1 HP.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder is attacked for regular damage that would faint it, this item has a 10% chance to prevent the holder’s HP from lowering below 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder may
endure a potential KO attack, leaving it with just
1 HP.

entries.name: Focus Sash
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder survives any single-hit attack at 1 HP if at max HP, then the item is consumed.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder has full HP and is attacked for regular damage that would faint it, this item is consumed and prevents the holder’s HP from lowering below 1.  This effect works against multi-hit attacks, but does not work against the effects of Doom Desire or Future Sight.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder has
full HP, it will endure a potential KO attack with
1 HP. The item then disappears.

entries.name: Forage Bag
entries.shortEffectText: Holds ingredients during Mallow’s trial.
entries.effectText: Holds ingredients during Mallow’s trial.
entries.flavorText:
A bag to carry ingredients gathered during Mallow’s
trial in the jungle.

entries.name: Fossilized Bird
entries.flavorText:
The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once soared
through the sky. What it looked like is a mystery.

entries.name: Fossilized Dino
entries.flavorText:
The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the
sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

entries.name: Fossilized Drake
entries.flavorText:
The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once roamed the
land. What it looked like is a mystery.

entries.name: Fossilized Fish
entries.flavorText:
The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the
sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

entries.name: Fresh Cream
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Add some whipped cream to a
spicy curry to give it some sweetness.

entries.name: Fresh Water
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 50 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 50 HP.
entries.flavorText:
Water with high mineral content. It can be used to
restore 30 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Fried Food
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. It’s been a while since it was
fried, so it’s gotten greasy.

entries.name: Friend Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Caught Pokémon start with 200 happiness. Made from Grn Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If caught, the wild Pokémon’s happiness starts at 200.
entries.flavorText:
A strange Poké Ball that will make the wild
Pokémon caught with it more friendly toward
you immediately.

entries.name: Fruit Bunch
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The syrupy fruits give off a strong
tropical feel.

entries.name: Full Heal
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine that is broadly effective.
It can be used to heal all the status conditions
of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Full Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder moves last in its priority bracket. Breeding: Snorlax begets a Munchlax Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder will go last within its move’s priority bracket, regardless of Speed.  If multiple Pokémon within the same priority bracket are subject to this effect, the slower Pokémon will go first.  The holder will move after Pokémon with Stall.  If the holder has Stall, Stall is ignored.  This item ignores Trick Room.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This
exotic-smelling incense makes the holder bloated
and slow moving.

entries.name: Full Restore
entries.shortEffectText: Restores HP to full and cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores HP to full and cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
A medicine that can be used to fully restore the HP of
a single Pokémon and heal any status conditions it has.

entries.name: Galactic Key
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to Galactic HQ in Veilstone City.
entries.effectText: Grants access to Galactic HQ in Veilstone City.
entries.flavorText:
A card key for disengaging the security systems in
the Galactic HQ. Losing it can result in
punishment, apparently.

entries.name: Galarica Cuff
entries.flavorText:
A cuff made from woven-together Galarica Twigs.
Giving it to Galarian Slowpoke makes the
Pokémon very happy.

entries.name: Galarica Twig
entries.flavorText:
A twig from a species of tree called Galarica that grows
in certain areas around the Galar region. It’s a material
for an accessory for a certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Galarica Wreath
entries.flavorText:
A wreath made from woven-together Galarica Twigs.
Placing it on the head of a Galarian Slowpoke makes
the Pokémon very happy.

entries.name: Galladite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Gallade to Mega Evolve into Mega Gallade.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Gallade to Mega Evolve into Mega Gallade.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Gallade hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Ganlon Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Defense.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its Defense by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its Defense stat will increase when it’s in
a pinch.

entries.name: Garchompite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Garchomp to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Garchomp to Mega Evolve into Mega Garchomp.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Garchomp hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Gardevoirite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Gardevoir to Mega Evolve into Mega Gardevoir.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Gardevoir to Mega Evolve into Mega Gardevoir.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Gardevoir hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Gastly Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: GB Sounds
entries.shortEffectText: Use to listen to GameBoy era audio.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer outside of battle
:   Changes the background music to the equivalent 8-bit music.
entries.flavorText:
A music player that allows you to listen to nostalgic
songs. It’s operated with the flip of a single switch.

entries.name: Gengarite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Gengar to Mega Evolve into Mega Gengar.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Gengar to Mega Evolve into Mega Gengar.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Gengar hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Genius Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases Special Attack effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s Special Attack effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the Sp. Atk base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Gentle Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Def will grow
more easily, but its Defense will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Geodude Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Ghost Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Ghost-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Ghost-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with a spectral essence. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Ghost-type move
one time.

entries.name: Ghost Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Ghost form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Ghost.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Ghost form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Ghost.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Ghost-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for ghostium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for ghostium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Ghost-type moves to Ghost-type Z-Moves.

entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Ghost moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Ghost moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Ghost-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Gigantamix
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. A mysterious spice. Even a tiny
sprinkle is sure to make a curry gigantic.

entries.name: Glalitite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Glalie to Mega Evolve into Mega Glalie.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Glalie to Mega Evolve into Mega Glalie.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Glalie hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

names.ja: キラーメのけっしょう

entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
PIKACHU print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Go-Goggles
entries.shortEffectText: Allows passage through windy deserts.
entries.effectText: Allows the trainer to enter the desert on Route 111.
entries.flavorText:
Nifty goggles to protect eyes from
desert sandstorms.

entries.name: god stone
entries.shortEffectText: Unknown.  Currently unused.
entries.effectText: Unknown.  Currently unused.
entries.flavorText: A rare stone.

names.ja: ゴールドチタンボトル

entries.name: Gold Bottle Cap
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Mr. Hyper to maximize all of a Pokémon’s genes.
entries.effectText: Trade to Mr. Hyper to set all of a Pokémon’s genes to 31.
entries.flavorText:
A beautiful bottle cap that gives off a golden gleam.
Some people are happy to receive one.

names.ja-hrkt: ゴールドチタンカップ
names.ja: ゴールドチタンカップ

entries.name: Gold Leaf
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious gold leaf. No trees with this leaf have
been found yet.

entries.name: Gold Teeth
entries.shortEffectText: The Safari Zone Warden’s dentures. Trade for HM04 (Strength).
entries.effectText: The Safari Zone warden’s teeth, to be returned to him for HM04.
entries.flavorText:
A set of golden false teeth lost by the Safari Zone’s
warden. They make his smile sparkle.

entries.name: Goldeen Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Golden Nanab Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch
drastically when given to them.

entries.name: Golden Pinap Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that makes you drastically more likely
to get an item when given to Pokémon you’re
trying to catch.

entries.name: Golden Razz Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that makes it drastically easier to catch
Pokémon when given to them.

entries.name: Good Rod
entries.shortEffectText: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
entries.effectText: Used to find Pokémon on the Good Rod list for an area, which are generally mediocre.
entries.flavorText:
A new, good-quality fishing rod. Use it at any body
of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

entries.name: Gooey Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Berries regrow from dead plants an increased number of times.
entries.effectText:
Used on a path of soil
:   Plant will regrow after dying 25% more times.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Gracidea
entries.shortEffectText: Changes an unfrozen Shaymin to Sky Forme in the day.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer on a Shaymin
:   Changes the target Shaymin from Land Forme to Sky Forme.

    This item cannot be used on a frozen Shaymin or at night.  Sky Forme Shaymin will revert to Land Forme overnight, when frozen, and upon entering a link battle.  This item must be used again to change it back.
entries.flavorText:
A flower sometimes bundled in bouquets to convey
gratitude on special occasions, such as birthdays.

entries.name: Gram 1
entries.shortEffectText: Part of a sidequest to obtain TM89.
entries.effectText: Give to the Wingull on Route 13, along with Gram 2 and Gram 3, to receive TM89.
entries.flavorText: An important letter that Wingull delivers.

entries.name: Gram 2
entries.shortEffectText: Part of a sidequest to obtain TM89.
entries.effectText: Give to the Wingull on Route 13, along with Gram 1 and Gram 3, to receive TM89.
entries.flavorText: An important letter that Wingull delivers.

entries.name: Gram 3
entries.shortEffectText: Part of a sidequest to obtain TM89.
entries.effectText: Give to the Wingull on Route 13, along with Gram 1 and Gram 2, to receive TM89.
entries.flavorText: An important letter that Wingull delivers.

Held

entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of nature. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Grass-type move
one time.

entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Grass form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Grass.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Grass-type data.

entries.effectText: XXX new effect for grassium-z--bag

entries.name: Grassy Seed
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed on Grassy Terrain and raises the holder’s Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder enters battle during Grassy Terrain, or if Grassy Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Defense raises by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Defense
on Grassy Terrain. It can only be used once.

entries.name: Great Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 1.5×.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1.5×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A good, high-performance Poké Ball that provides
a higher success rate for catching Pokémon than
a standard Poké Ball.

entries.name: Green Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Friend Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Friend Ball.
entries.flavorText:
A green Apricorn. It has a mysterious,
aromatic scent.

entries.name: Green Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for green-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for green-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Mallow
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Green Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Smartness while in a contest.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Smartness during a Super Contest’s Visual Competition.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest,
it raises the Clever aspect of the Pokémon
holding it.

entries.name: Green Shard
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
entries.effectText:
No effect.

In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a Hail TM in the house midway along the southern section of Route 212.

In Platinum, trade to move tutors on Route 212, in Snowpoint City, and in the Survival Area.  Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for an Aguav Berry, a Lum Berry, and a Rawst Berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in Violet City.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Durin Berry, a Hondew Berry, and a Wepear Berry with the Juggler near the Pal Park entrance in Fuchsia City.
entries.flavorText:
A small green shard. It appears to be a fragment of
some sort of implement made long ago.

entries.name: Greet Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to be easy to use when writing
someone for the first time. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Grepa Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops Special Defense Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers Special Defense effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
Sp. Def base points.

entries.name: Grimer Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Grip Claw
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder’s multi-turn trapping moves last 5 turns.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the duration of the holder’s multiturn (2-5 turn) moves by three turns.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of multi-turn attacks, such as Bind and Wrap.

entries.name: Griseous Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Boosts the damage from Giratina’s Dragon-type and Ghost-type moves by 20%, and transforms it into Origin Forme.
entries.effectText:
Held by Giratina
:   Holder’s Dragon and Ghost moves have 1.2× their base power.

    Holder is in Origin Forme.

This item cannot be held by any Pokémon but Giratina.  When you enter the Union Room or connect to Wi-Fi, this item returns to your bag.
entries.flavorText:
A glowing orb to be held by Giratina. It boosts the
power of Dragon- and Ghost-type moves when it
is held.

entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Ground-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Ground-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of land. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Ground-type move
one time.

entries.flavorText:

entries.effectText: XXX new effect for groundium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:

entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Ground moves.

entries.name: Growlithe Candy

A candy that is packed with energy. When given to

entries.name: Growth Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Growing time of berries is reduced, but the soil dries out faster.
entries.effectText:
Used on a patch of soil
:   Plant’s growth stages will each last 25% less time.  Dries soil out more quickly.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Grubby Hanky
entries.shortEffectText: Appears in the Café Warehouse on Sunday; return to the customer with a Patrat on Thursday.
entries.flavorText:
A handkerchief dropped by a regular at
Café Warehouse. It smells faintly like a Pokémon.

entries.name: Guard Spec.
entries.shortEffectText: Prevents stat changes in battle for five turns in battle.  Raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Protects the target’s stats from being lowered for the next five turns.  Increases happiness by 1/1/0.
entries.flavorText:
An item that prevents stat reduction among the
Trainer’s party Pokémon for five turns after it is
used in battle.

entries.name: Gyaradosite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Gyarados to Mega Evolve into Mega Gyarados.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Gyarados to Mega Evolve into Mega Gyarados.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Gyarados hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Haban Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Dragon-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Dragon-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Dragon-type attack.

entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Rock moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a durable
stone that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Speed will grow

entries.name: Heal Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Caught Pokémon are immediately healed.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×.  The caught Pokémon’s HP is immediately restored, PP for all its moves is restored, and any status ailment is cured.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A remedial Poké Ball that restores the HP of a
Pokémon caught with it and eliminates any
status conditions.

entries.name: Heal Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any status ailment, but lowers happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any status ailment.  Decreases happiness by 5/5/10.
entries.flavorText:
A very bitter medicinal powder. It can be used once
to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Health Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s HP.

entries.name: Health Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase
that Pokémon’s HP.

entries.name: Health Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase
that Pokémon’s HP.

entries.name: Health Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases HP effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s HP effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the HP base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Heart Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of giant
heart patterns.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Heart Scale
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Can be traded for prior Level-up moves.
entries.effectText: Trade one to the Move Relearner near the shore in Pastoria City or with the Move Deleter in Blackthorn City to teach one party Pokémon a prior level-up move.
entries.flavorText:
A pretty, heart-shaped scale that is extremely
rare. Some people are happy to receive one.

entries.name: Heat Rock
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Sunny Day by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder’s Sunny Day will create sunshine lasting for eight turns rather than five.  As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of the move Sunny Day when used by
the holder.

entries.name: Heavy Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Has flat bonus or penalty to catch rate depending on weight class of target. Made from Blk Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If the wild Pokémon weighs:

    * 409.6 kg (903.0 lb) or more, its catch rate is 40 more than normal.
    * 307.2 kg (677.3 lb) or more, its catch rate is 30 more than normal.
    * 204.8 kg (451.5 lb) or more, its catch rate is 20 more than normal.
    * less than 204.8 kg (451.5 lb), its catch rate is 20 less than normal.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that is better than usual at catching
very heavy Pokémon.

entries.name: Heavy-Duty Boots
entries.flavorText:
These boots prevent the effects of traps set on
the battlefield.

entries.name: Helix Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into an Omanyte.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive an Omanyte.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sea. It might be a piece of a seashell.

entries.name: Heracronite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Heracross to Mega Evolve into Mega Heracross.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Heracross to Mega Evolve into Mega Heracross.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Heracross hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

names.ja: ゆうしゃのけんピック

entries.flavorText:
Strange earbuds that allow you to freely control the
volume of various sounds.

A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: HM01
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Cut to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Cut to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Fly to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Fly to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.name: HM03
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Surf to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Surf to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.name: HM04
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Strength to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Strength to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Waterfall to a compatible Pokémon. (HS: Whirlpool DPP: Defog Gen III & II & I: Flash)
entries.effectText: Teaches Defog to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Dive to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Rock Smash Gen II: Whirlpool)
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Smash to a compatible Pokémon.

entries.effectText: Teaches Waterfall to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Diving on the first turn, the user floats up and
attacks on the next turn.

entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a move to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Rock Climb Gen III: Dive)
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Climb to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A charging attack that may also leave
the foe confused. It can also be used
to scale rocky walls.

entries.name: Holo Caster
entries.shortEffectText: Displays cutscene conversations as the plot advances.
entries.effectText: Displays cutscene conversations as the plot advances.
entries.flavorText:
A device that allows users to receive and view
hologram clips at any time. It’s also used to chat
with others.

entries.name: Holo Caster
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for holo-caster--green
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for holo-caster--green
entries.flavorText:
A device that allows users to receive and view
hologram clips at any time. It’s also used to chat
with others.

entries.name: Holo Caster
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for holo-caster--red
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for holo-caster--red
entries.flavorText:
A device that allows users to receive and view
hologram clips at any time. It’s also used to chat
with others.

entries.name: Hondew Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops Special Attack Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers Special Attack effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
Sp. Atk base points.

entries.name: Honey
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Immediately triggers a wild Pokémon battle, as long as the trainer is somewhere with wild Pokémon—i.e., in tall grass, in a cave, or surfing.

Can be smeared on sweet-smelling trees to attract tree-dwelling Pokémon after six hours.
entries.flavorText:
A sweet honey collected by Pokémon. It has a lush
aroma and can be sold at a low price to shops.

entries.name: Honor of Kalos
entries.shortEffectText: Does nothing, but signifies becoming Champion.
entries.effectText: Does nothing, but signifies becoming Champion.
entries.flavorText:
A precious symbol that is awarded only to an
individual who has done great things for the
Kalos region.

entries.name: Horsea Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Houndoominite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Houndoom to Mega Evolve into Mega Houndoom.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Houndoom to Mega Evolve into Mega Houndoom.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Houndoom hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: HP Up
entries.shortEffectText: Raises HP effort and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases HP effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed,
it raises the HP base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Hyper Potion
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 200 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 200 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can
be used to restore 120 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Iapapa Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike sour flavor.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP.  If the holder dislikes sour flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Defense), it will also become confused.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a
pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates
the taste.

entries.name: Ice Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Ice-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Ice-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of ice. When held, it
strengthens the power of an Ice-type move
one time.

entries.name: Ice Heal
entries.shortEffectText: Cures freezing.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures freezing.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating freezing. It can be
used to thaw out a single Pokémon that has been
frozen solid.

entries.name: Ice Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Ice form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Ice.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Ice form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Ice.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Ice-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Ice Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves an Alola Sandshrew into Alola Sandslash or an Alola Vulpix into Alola Ninetales.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves an Alola Sandshrew into Alola Sandslash or an Alola Vulpix into Alola Ninetales.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It has an unmistakable
snowflake pattern.

entries.name: Iceroot Carrot
entries.flavorText:
Produce that the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved
steed likes to eat. It grew in a field covered in snow.

entries.name: Icicle Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ice-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ice.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ice moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Ice.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Ice-type moves.

entries.name: Icium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for icium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for icium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Ice-type moves to Ice-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Icium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Ice moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Ice moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Ice-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Icy Rock
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Hail by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder’s Hail will create a hailstorm lasting for eight turns rather than five.  As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of the move Hail when used by
the holder.

entries.name: Ilima Normalium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for ilimas-normalium-z
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for ilimas-normalium-z
entries.flavorText:
The Normalium Z you were entrusted with by Ilima.
You were asked to place it on the pedestal at
the back of Verdant Cavern.

entries.name: Impish Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Defense will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Atk will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Incinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for incinium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for incinium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Incineroar’s Darkest Lariat to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Incinium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Incineroar to upgrade Darkest Lariat into Malicious Moonsault.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Incineroar to upgrade Darkest Lariat into Malicious Moonsault.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Incineroar’s Darkest Lariat to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Inquiry Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to make it easier to pose a
question about something. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Insect Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Bug-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Bug.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Bug moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Bug.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Bug-type moves.

entries.name: Instant Noodles
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. It has a junk-food sort of taste,
but it goes well with curry.

entries.name: Intriguing Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Trade for a Sun Stone in X and Y, or Pidgeotite in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire.
entries.effectText: Trade for a Sun Stone in X and Y, or Pidgeotite in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire.
entries.flavorText:
A rather curious stone that might appear valuable
to some. It’s all in the eye of the beholder.

entries.name: Iron
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Defense effort and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases Defense effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed, it
raises the Defense base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Iron Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder’s Speed is halved. Negates all Ground-type immunities, and makes Flying-types take neutral damage from Ground-type moves. Arena Trap. Spikes, and Toxic Spikes affect the holder.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  If the holder is Flying or has Levitate, it takes regular damage from Ground attacks and is suspectible to Spikes and Toxic Spikes.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It lowers Speed
and allows Ground-type moves to hit Flying types
and holders that are levitating.

entries.name: Iron Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Steel-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Steel.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Steel moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Steel.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Steel-type moves.

entries.name: Item Drop
entries.shortEffectText: Forces a friendly Pokémon to drop its held item.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Selects another friendly Pokémon at random.  If that Pokémon is holding an item, that item is removed for the duration of the battle.

This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to drop a
held item.

entries.name: Item Urge
entries.shortEffectText: Forcibly activates a friendly Pokémon’s held item.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Selects another friendly Pokémon at random.  If that Pokémon is holding an item normally activated by some condition—i.e., not continuous and passive—its effect is forcibly activated.

This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
When used, it causes an ally Pokémon to use its
held item.

entries.name: Jaboca Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed to deal 1/8 attacker’s max HP when holder is struck by a physical attack.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder takes physical damage, it consumes this item to damage the attacking Pokémon for 1/8 its max HP.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a physical
attack hits the Pokémon holding it, the attacker will
also be hurt.

entries.name: Jade Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Rayquaza to the Embedded Tower.
entries.effectText: Causes Rayquaza to appear in the Embedded Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A shiny green orb that is said to have a legend tied
to it. It’s known to have a deep connection with the
Hoenn region.

entries.name: Jaw Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Tyrunt.
entries.effectText: Can be revived into a Tyrunt.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
on the land. It looks as if it could be a piece of a
large jaw.

entries.name: Jigglypuff Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Jolly Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Speed will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Atk will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Journal
entries.shortEffectText: Records prior significant activities the player took.
entries.effectText: Records some of the trainer’s activities for the day.
entries.flavorText:
A notebook that keeps a day-to-day record of your
adventure so far.

entries.name: Jynx Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Kabuto Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Kangaskhan Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Kangaskhanite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Kangaskhan to Mega Evolve into Mega Kangaskhan.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Kangaskhan to Mega Evolve into Mega Kangaskhan.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Kangaskhan hold it, and this stone will enable
it to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Kasib Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Ghost-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Ghost-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Ghost-type attack.

entries.name: Kebia Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Poison-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Poison-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Poison-type attack.

entries.name: Kee Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a physical move, increases its Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a physical move, increases its Defense by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will increase the
holder’s Defense if it’s hit with a physical move.

entries.name: Kelpsy Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops Attack Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers Attack effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
Attack base points.

entries.name: Key Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.effectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.flavorText:
A stone filled with an unexplained power. It makes
Pokémon that battle with a Mega Stone
Mega Evolve.

entries.name: Key to Room 1
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks the door to Room 1 in Sea Mauville.
entries.effectText: Unlocks the door to Room 1 in Sea Mauville.
entries.flavorText: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.

entries.name: Key to Room 2
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks the door to Room 2 in Sea Mauville.
entries.effectText: Unlocks the door to Room 2 in Sea Mauville.
entries.flavorText: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.

entries.name: Key to Room 4
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks the door to Room 4 in Sea Mauville.
entries.effectText: Unlocks the door to Room 4 in Sea Mauville.
entries.flavorText: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.

entries.name: Key to Room 6
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks the door to Room 6 in Sea Mauville.
entries.effectText: Unlocks the door to Room 6 in Sea Mauville.
entries.flavorText: A key that opens a door inside Sea Mauville.

entries.name: King’s Rock
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Damaging moves gain a 10% chance to make their target flinch. Traded on a Poliwhirl: Holder evolves into Politoed. Traded on a Slowpoke: Holder evolves into Slowking.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   Holder’s damaging moves have a 10% chance to make their target flinch.  This chance applies independently to each hit of a multi-hit move.

    This item’s chance is rolled independently of any other move effects;
e.g., a move with a 30% chance to flinch normally will have a 37% total chance to flinch when used with this item, because 3% of the time, both effects activate.

Held by Poliwhirl or Slowbro
:   Holder evolves into Politoed or Slowking, respectively, when traded.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder
successfully inflicts damage, the target may
also flinch.

entries.name: Koffing Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Kommonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for kommonium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for kommonium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Kommo-o’s Clanging Scales to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Kommonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for kommonium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for kommonium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Kommo-o’s Clanging Scales to
a Z-Move.

entries.name: Krabby Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

names.ja-hrkt: コロボーシのぬけがら
names.ja: コロボーシのぬけがら

entries.name: Feather Ball
entries.flavorText:
Can be thrown further than a regular Poké Ball.
Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.

entries.name: Lagging Tail
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder moves last in its priority bracket.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder will go last within its move’s priority bracket, regardless of Speed.  If multiple Pokémon within the same priority bracket are subject to this effect, the slower Pokémon will go first.  The holder will move after Pokémon with Stall.  If the holder has Stall, Stall is ignored.  This item ignores Trick Room.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is tremendously
heavy and makes the holder move slower
than usual.

entries.name: Gigaton Ball
entries.flavorText:
Upgraded version of the Leaden Ball. Doesn't fly far,
but is more effective if the Pokémon hasn't noticed the player.

entries.name: Heavy Ball
entries.flavorText:
Doesn't fly far, but is more effective if the Pokémon
hasn't noticed the player.

entries.name: Jet Ball
entries.flavorText:
Can be thrown further than a Wing Ball.
Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.

entries.name: Leaden Ball
entries.flavorText:
Upgraded version of the Heavy Ball. Doesn't fly far,
but is more effective if the Pokémon hasn't noticed the player.

entries.name: Lansat Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost critical hit ratio by two stages.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its critical hit chance by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its critical-hit ratio will increase when it’s
in a pinch.

entries.name: Lapras Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Large Leek
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Whether this is the kind of
vegetable stalk that Farfetch’d like is unknown.

entries.name: Latiasite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Latias to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Latias to Mega Evolve into Mega Latias.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Latias hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Latiosite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Latios to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Latios to Mega Evolve into Mega Latios.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Latios hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Lava Cookie
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
Lavaridge Town’s local specialty. It can be used
once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Wing Ball
entries.flavorText:
Can be thrown further than a Feather Ball.
Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.

entries.name: Lax Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder’s evasion is increased by 5%. Breeding: Wobbuffet begets a Wynaut Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Evasion by 5%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The beguiling
aroma of this incense may cause attacks to miss
its holder.

entries.name: Lax Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Defense will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Def will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Leaf Letter
entries.flavorText:
A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using
Eevee’s footprints.

entries.name: Leaf Letter
entries.flavorText:
A letter written on a leaf. Something is written using
Pikachu’s footprints.

entries.name: Leaf Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves an Exeggcute into Exeggutor, a Gloom into Vileplume, a Nuzleaf into Shiftry, a Pansage into Simisage, or a Weepinbell into Victreebel.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves an Exeggcute into Exeggutor, a Gloom into Vileplume, a Nuzleaf into Shiftry, a Pansage into Simisage, or a Weepinbell into Victreebel.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It has an unmistakable leaf pattern.

entries.name: Left Poké Ball
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for left-poke-ball
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for left-poke-ball
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball with a Pokémon that lost its Trainer
inside. It seems to be from Ula’ula Island.

entries.name: Leftovers
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Restores 1/16 (6.25%) holder’s max HP at the end of each turn.
entries.effectText: Held: Heals the holder by 1/16 its max HP at the end of each turn.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder’s HP
is slowly but steadily restored throughout
every battle.

entries.name: Legendary Clue 1
entries.flavorText:
A clue about the King of Bountiful Harvests. It’s written
by Peony, and an old photo is attached to it.

entries.name: Legendary Clue 2
entries.flavorText:
A clue about the legend of giants. It’s written
by Peony and has unique drawings.

entries.name: Legendary Clue 3
entries.flavorText:
A clue about the legend of bird Pokémon. It’s written
by Peony and has a unique drawing.

entries.name: Legendary Clue?
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious clue that Peony seems to have dropped.
There’s a photo attached to it showing a shining hole
opening above the Max Lair.

entries.name: Lemonade
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 80 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 80 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A very sweet and refreshing drink. It can be used to
restore 70 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Lens Case
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to change their eye color.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to change their eye color.
entries.flavorText:
A rather chic-looking case for carrying
contact lenses.

entries.name: Leppa Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when a move runs out of PP to restore its PP by 10.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is out of PP for one of its moves, it consumes this item to restore 10 of that move’s PP.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 10 PP for a selected move.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can restore 10 PP to a depleted move
during battle.

entries.name: Letter
entries.shortEffectText: A letter to Steven from the Devon Corp president.
entries.effectText: Contains a letter to be delivered to Steven.
entries.flavorText:
An extremely important letter to Steven from
the president of Devon Corporation.

entries.name: Level Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Success rate based off of fraction target Pokémon is of user’s Pokémon. Made from Red Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If the trainer’s Pokémon’s level is higher than:

    * four times the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 8× normal.
    * than twice the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 4× normal.
    * the wild Pokémon’s, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 2× normal.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon
that are at a lower level than your own Pokémon.

entries.name: Liberty Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Liberty Garden and Victini.
entries.effectText: Allows passage on the Castelia City ship, which leads to Liberty Garden and Victini.
entries.flavorText:
A special pass to go to Liberty Garden.
Board the ship in Castelia City.

entries.name: Lickitung Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Liechi Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Attack.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its Attack by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its Attack stat will increase when it’s in
a pinch.

entries.name: Life Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder’s moves inflict 30% extra damage, but cost 10% max HP.
entries.effectText: Held: Damage from the holder’s moves is increased by 30%.  On each turn the holder uses a damage-inflicting move, it takes 10% its max HP in damage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the
power of moves but at the cost of some HP on
each hit.

entries.name: Lift Key
entries.shortEffectText: Operates the elevator in Team Rocket’s Celadon Hideout.
entries.effectText: Operates the elevator in the Celadon Rocket Hideout.
entries.flavorText:
A key that operates the elevator in the Team Rocket
Hideout. It bears the Team Rocket logo.

entries.name: Light Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles Pikachu’s Attack and Special Attack. Breed on Pikachu or Raichu: Pichu Egg will have Volt Tackle.
entries.effectText: Held by Pikachu: Doubles the holder’s initial Attack and Special Attack.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Pikachu. It’s a puzzling orb
