that boosts its Attack and Sp. Atk stats.

entries.name: Light Clay
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Light Screen and Reflect used by the holder last 8 rounds instead of 5.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder’s Reflect and Light Screen will create effects lasting for eight turns rather than five.  As this item affects the move rather than the barrier itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. Protective moves
like Light Screen and Reflect will be
effective longer.

entries.name: Light Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Reshiram for the final battle against N.
entries.effectText: Summons Reshiram for the final battle against N.
entries.flavorText:
Reshiram’s body was destroyed and changed into
this stone. It is said to be waiting for the
emergence of a hero.

entries.name: Like Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to allow you to recommend
something to the reader. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Linking Cord
entries.shortEffectText: Allows a Pokemon whose evolution is usually triggered by trading to evolve
entries.effectText: The Linking Cord triggers the evolution of certain Pokémon (all of which can alternatively evolve when traded) upon use. This consumes the Linking Cord.

entries.name: Lock Capsule
entries.shortEffectText: Contains TM95 (Snarl).
entries.effectText: Does nothing.
entries.flavorText:
A sturdy Capsule that can only be opened with a
special key.

entries.name: Lone Earring
entries.flavorText: A single earring that somebody dropped.

entries.name: Lonely Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Attack will grow
more easily, but its Defense will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Looker Ticket
entries.shortEffectText: Advances the Looker postgame plot.
entries.effectText: Advances the Looker postgame plot.
entries.flavorText:
A ticket that was handmade by Looker. It’s
decorated with a liberal amount of glittery paint.

entries.name: Loot Sack
entries.shortEffectText: Carries coal mine loot.
entries.effectText: Unused.
entries.flavorText:
A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry any
treasures or loot obtained in the coal mine.

entries.name: Lopunnite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Lopunny to Mega Evolve into Mega Lopunny.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Lopunny to Mega Evolve into Mega Lopunny.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Lopunny hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Lost Item
entries.shortEffectText: A Poké Doll lost by the Copycat who lives in Saffron City. Trade for a Pass.
entries.effectText: May be traded to the Copycat for a Pass.
entries.flavorText: The Mime Jr. doll that was lost by the Copycat.

entries.name: Love Ball
entries.shortEffectText: 8× effectiveness on opposite sex, same species targets of the Active Pokémon. Made from Pnk Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If the trainer’s Pokémon and wild Pokémon are of the same species but opposite genders, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 8× normal.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that works best when catching a
Pokémon that is of the opposite gender of
your Pokémon.

entries.name: Love Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A heart-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Lucarionite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Lucario to Mega Evolve into Mega Lucario.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Lucario to Mega Evolve into Mega Lucario.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Lucario hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Luck Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Doubles the money earned from a battle. Does not stack with Amulet Coin. Breeding: Chansey and Blissey beget a Happiny Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Doubles the money the trainer receives after an in-game trainer battle.  This effect cannot apply more than once to the same battle.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles any
prize money received if the holding Pokémon joins
a battle.

entries.name: Lucky Egg
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Increases EXP earned in battle by 50%.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases any Exp the holder gains by 50%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s an egg filled
with happiness that earns the holder extra Exp. Points
in battle.

entries.name: Lucky Punch
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Chansey’s critical hit ratio by two stages.
entries.effectText: Held by Chansey: Raises the holder’s critical hit counter by 2.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Chansey. This pair of lucky
boxing gloves will boost Chansey’s critical-hit ratio.

entries.name: Lum Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed to cure any status condition or confusion.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is afflicted with a major status ailment, it consumes this item to cure the ailment.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any major status ailment.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from any status condition
during battle.

entries.name: Luminous Moss
entries.shortEffectText: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Water move, raises its Special Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Water move, it consumes this item and raises its Special Defense by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def
if the holder is hit with a Water-type attack. It can only
be used once.

entries.name: Lumiose Galette
entries.shortEffectText: Cures all major status ailments and confusion.
entries.effectText: Cures all major status ailments and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
A popular treat in Lumiose City. It can be used once
to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Lunalium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for lunalium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for lunalium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Lunala’s Moongeist Beam to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Lunalium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for lunalium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for lunalium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Lunala’s Moongeist Beam to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Lunar Wing
entries.shortEffectText: Cures sailor’s son of nightmares in Canalave City.
entries.effectText: Cures the sailor’s son of his nightmares; no reward, only a side effect of seeing Cresselia.
entries.flavorText:
A feather that glows like the moon. It’s said to
possess the power to dispel nightmares.

entries.name: Lure
entries.flavorText:
A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon
more likely to appear.

entries.name: Lure Ball
entries.shortEffectText: 3× effectiveness while fishing. Made from Blu Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If the wild Pokémon was encountered by fishing, the wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 3× normal.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that is good for catching Pokémon that
you reel in with a rod while out fishing.

entries.name: Lustrous Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Boosts the damage from Palkia’s Dragon-type and Water-type moves by 20%.
entries.effectText:
Held by Palkia
:   Holder’s Dragon- and Water-type moves have 1.2× their usual power.
entries.flavorText:
A beautifully glowing orb to be held by Palkia.
It boosts the power of Dragon- and Water-type
moves when it is held.

entries.name: Luxury Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Caught Pokémon start with 200 happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×.  Whenever the caught Pokémon’s happiness increases, it increases by one extra point.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A particularly comfortable Poké Ball that makes a
wild Pokémon quickly grow friendlier after
being caught.

entries.name: Lycanium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for lycanium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for lycanium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Lycanroc’s Stone Edge to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Lycanium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for lycanium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for lycanium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Lycanroc’s Stone Edge to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Mach Bike
entries.shortEffectText: Faster than the Acro Bike, and can ride up sandy slopes and across cracked floors.
entries.effectText: Increases movement speed outside or in caves.  Faster than the Acro Bike.  Allows the trainer to ascend muddy slopes.
entries.flavorText:
This folding bike more than doubles your
movement speed.

entries.name: Machine Part
entries.shortEffectText: Must be replaced in the Power Plant to power the Magnet Train.
entries.effectText: Must be replaced in the Kanto Power Plant to power the Magnet Train.
entries.flavorText:
An important part of a machine that was stolen
from the Power Plant.

entries.name: Macho Brace
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains double effort values from battles, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder would gain effort due to battle, it gains double that effort instead.

Held in battle
:   Holder has half its Speed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This stiff, heavy
brace helps Pokémon grow strong but cuts Speed
in battle.

entries.name: Machop Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

names.ja-hrkt: マジカルハートピック
names.ja: マジカルハートピック

names.ja-hrkt: マジカルスターピック
names.ja: マジカルスターピック

entries.name: Magikarp Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Magma Emblem
entries.shortEffectText: Provides access to the Team Magma Hideout in the Jagged Pass.
entries.effectText: Provides access to the Magma Hideout in the Jagged Pass.
entries.flavorText:
A medal-like item in
the same shape as
TEAM MAGMA’s mark.

entries.name: Magma Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Magma is sealed inside.
entries.effectText:
Causes Heatran to appear at Reversal Mountain.

Unused prior to Black and White 2.
entries.flavorText:
A stone formed by boulders melting in intensely hot
magma, then hardening. Magma remains sealed
inside it.

entries.name: Magma Suit
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to ride Groudon in the Cave of Origin.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to ride Groudon in the Cave of Origin.
entries.flavorText:
A suit made with the collective technological
know-how of Team Magma. It can withstand
any impact.

entries.name: Magmar Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Magmarizer
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Magmar: Holder evolves into Magmortar.
entries.effectText: Held by Magmar: Evolves the holder into Magmortar when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A box packed with a tremendous amount of magma
energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Magnemite Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Magnet
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Electric-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Electric moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a powerful
magnet that boosts the power of
Electric-type moves.

entries.name: Mago Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike sweet flavor.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP.  If the holder dislikes sweet flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Speed), it will also become confused.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a
pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates
the taste.

entries.name: Magost Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cuteness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Makeup Bag
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to change their lipstick color.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to change their lipstick color.
entries.flavorText: A rather chic-looking case for carrying lipsticks.

entries.name: Manectite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Manectric to Mega Evolve into Mega Manectric.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Manectric to Mega Evolve into Mega Manectric.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Manectric hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Mankey Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Maranga Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a special move, increases its Special Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a special move, increases its Special Defense by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will increase the
holder’s Sp. Def if it’s hit with a special move.

entries.name: Marble
entries.flavorText:
A round glass marble. You can see colored glass
inside the transparent marble.

entries.name: Mark Charm
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious and unique charm that increases the
chance of finding a Pokémon with a mark.

entries.name: Marshadium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for marshadium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for marshadium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Marshadow’s Spectral Thief to an
exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Marshadium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Marshadow to upgrade Spectral Thief into Soul-Stealing 7 Star Strike.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Marshadow to upgrade Spectral Thief into Soul-Stealing 7-Star Strike.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Marshadow’s Spectral Thief to
a Z-Move.

entries.name: Master Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Catches a wild Pokémon every time.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Catches a wild Pokémon without fail.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
The best Poké Ball with the ultimate level of
performance. With it, you will catch any wild
Pokémon without fail.

entries.name: Mawilite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Mawile to Mega Evolve into Mega Mawile.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Mawile to Mega Evolve into Mega Mawile.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Mawile hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Max Elixir
entries.shortEffectText: Restores PP to full for each move.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores PP to full for each move.
entries.flavorText:
This medicine can be used to fully restore the PP of all
of the moves that have been learned by a Pokémon.

entries.name: Max Ether
entries.shortEffectText: Restores PP to full for one move.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores PP to full for a selected move.
entries.flavorText:
This medicine can be used to fully restore the PP of
a single selected move that has been learned by
a Pokémon.

entries.name: Max Honey
entries.flavorText:
Honey that Dynamax Vespiquen produces. Adding this
honey to Max Soup makes the taste very smooth. It also
has the same effect as a Max Revive.

entries.name: Max Lure
entries.flavorText:
A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon
more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Super Lure.

entries.name: Max Mushrooms
entries.flavorText:
Mushrooms that have the power of changing Dynamax
forms. They boost all stats of a Pokémon during battle.

entries.name: Max Potion
entries.shortEffectText: Restores HP to full.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores HP to full.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can
be used to completely restore the max HP of
a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Max Repel
entries.shortEffectText: For 250 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon.  This effect wears off after the trainer takes 250 steps.
entries.flavorText:
An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon
from jumping out at you for a while. It lasts longer
than Super Repel.

entries.name: Max Revive
entries.shortEffectText: Revives with full HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Revives the Pokémon and restores its HP to full.
entries.flavorText:
A medicine that can be used to revive a single
Pokémon that has fainted. It also fully restores
the Pokémon’s max HP.

entries.name: Meadow Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Grass.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Grass moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Grass.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

entries.name: Mech Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
MAGNEMITE print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Medal Box
entries.shortEffectText: Holds medals recieved in the medal rally.
entries.flavorText:
A box-shaped machine that stores Medals and
Medal information.

entries.name: Medichamite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Medicham to Mega Evolve into Mega Medicham.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Medicham to Mega Evolve into Mega Medicham.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Medicham hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Mega Anchor
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Archie’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This anchor contains an untold power that
somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone
to Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Anklet
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Zinnia’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This anklet contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Bracelet
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.effectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.flavorText:
This cuff contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Charm
entries.shortEffectText: Unused Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This appears as the girlplayer’s Mega Bracelet in Pokémon Contests, but it cannot actually be obtained.
entries.flavorText:
This charm contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Cuff
entries.shortEffectText: Unused Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This appears as the boy player’s Mega Bracelet in Pokémon Contests, but it cannot actually be obtained.
entries.flavorText:
This bracelet contains an untold power that
somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone
to Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Glasses
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Maxie’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
These glasses contain an untold power that
somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone
to Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Glove
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Korrina’s Key Stone in X and Y, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This glove contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Pendant
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Wally’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This necklace contains an untold power that
somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone
to Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Ring
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.effectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to Mega Evolve.
entries.flavorText:
This ring contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Stickpin
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Steven’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This stickpin contains an untold power that
somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone
to Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Mega Tiara
entries.shortEffectText: Unused NPC Key Stone.
entries.effectText: Unused.  This is Lisia’s Key Stone in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire, but it cannot be obtained by the player.
entries.flavorText:
This tiara contains an untold power that somehow
enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to
Mega Evolve in battle.

entries.name: Meltan Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Member Card
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Newmoon Island and Darkrai.
entries.effectText: Provides access to Newmoon Island and Darkrai.
entries.flavorText:
A card needed for entering the inn in Canalave City.
Oddly, the last date marked on it was
50 years ago.

entries.name: Mental Herb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed to cure infatuation. Gen V: Also removes Taunt, Encore, Torment, Disable, and Cursed Body.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is attracted, it consumes this item to cure the attraction.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder
shakes off move-binding effects to move freely.
It can be used only once.

entries.name: Meowth Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Metagrossite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Metagross to Mega Evolve into Mega Metagross.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Metagross to Mega Evolve into Mega Metagross.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Metagross hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Metal Coat
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Steel-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Steel moves by 20%.
Held by Onix or Scyther: Evolves the holder into Steelix or Scizor when traded, respectively.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a special
metallic film that can boost the power of
Steel-type moves.

entries.name: Metal Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Ditto’s Defense and Special Defense by 50%. The boost is lost after transforming.
entries.effectText: Held by Ditto: Increases the holder’s initial Defense and Special Defense by 50%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Ditto. Extremely fine yet
hard, this odd powder boosts the Defense stat.

entries.name: Meteorite
entries.shortEffectText: FL: Deliver to Lostelle’s father. RSE: Trade to Professor Cozmo for TM27 (Return).
entries.effectText:
RSE: May be traded to Professor Cozmo for TM27.

FRLG: A meteorite to be delivered to Lostelle’s father.
entries.flavorText:
A Meteorite originally found at Meteor Falls.
You got it at Mt. Chimney.

entries.name: Meteorite
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--2
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--2
entries.flavorText:
A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney. It gives off
a faint glow.

entries.name: Meteorite
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--3
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--3
entries.flavorText:
A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney.
It continues to give off a faint glow and is slightly
warm to the touch.

entries.name: Meteorite
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--4
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for meteorite--4
entries.flavorText:
A Meteorite that you got at Mt. Chimney.
Some sort of pattern has risen to the surface,
and it’s shining with seven colors of light.

entries.name: Meteorite Shard
entries.shortEffectText: Causes the Meteorite to transform to its final form, allowing Rayquaza to Mega Evolve.
entries.effectText: Causes the Meteorite to transform to its final form, allowing Rayquaza to Mega Evolve.
entries.flavorText:
One of the fragments of a Meteorite from
Granite Cave. It’s faintly warm to the touch.

entries.name: Metronome
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consectutive uses of the same attack have a cumulative damage boost of 10%. Maximum 100% boost.
entries.effectText: Held: Each time the holder uses the same move consecutively, its power is increased by another 10% of its original, to a maximum of 100%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts moves
used consecutively but only until a different move
is used.

entries.name: Mew Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Mewnium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for mewnium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for mewnium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Mew’s Psychic to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Mewnium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Mew to upgrade Psychic into Genesis Supernova.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Mew to upgrade Psychic into Genesis Supernova.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Mew’s Psychic to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Mewtwo Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Mewtwonite X
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Mewtwo to Mega Evolve into Mega Mewtwo X.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Mewtwo to Mega Evolve into Mega Mewtwo X.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Mewtwo hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Mewtwonite Y
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Mewtwo to Mega Evolve into Mega Mewtwo Y.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Mewtwo to Mega Evolve into Mega Mewtwo Y.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Mewtwo hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Micle Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost accuracy of next move by 20%. (Gen IV: Perfect accuracy)
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item, and its next used move has 1.2× its normal accuracy.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its accuracy will increase just once when
it’s in a pinch.

entries.name: Mighty Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Attack stat.

entries.name: Mighty Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Attack stat.

entries.name: Mighty Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Attack stat.

entries.name: Mild Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Atk will grow
more easily, but its Defense will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Mimikium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for mimikium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for mimikium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Mimikyu’s Play Rough to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Mimikium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for mimikium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for mimikium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Mimikyu’s Play Rough to a Z-Move.

names.ja-hrkt: ミミッキュのきれはし
names.ja: ミミッキュのきれはし

entries.name: Mind Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Psychic.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Psychic moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Psychic.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Psychic-type moves.

entries.name: Miracle Seed
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Grass moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a seed
imbued with life-force that boosts the power of
Grass-type moves.

entries.name: Misty Seed
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed on Misty Terrain and raises the holder’s Special Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder enters battle during Misty Terrain, or if Misty Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Special Defense raises by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def
on Misty Terrain. It can only be used once.

entries.name: Mixed Mushrooms
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The chewy texture of the
mushrooms transforms any dish they’re added to.

entries.name: Modest Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Atk will grow
more easily, but its Attack will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Moltres Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Moomoo Cheese
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Melted cheese can really give a
curry some richness.

entries.name: Moomoo Milk
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 100 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 100 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A bottle of highly nutritious milk. It can be used to
restore 100 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Moon Ball
entries.shortEffectText: 4× effectiveness on familes of Pokémon with a Moon Stone evolution. Made from Ylw Apricorn.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.

    If the wild Pokémon is a Clefairy, Nidoran♂, Nidoran♀, Jigglypuff, Skitty, or any evolution thereof, the wild Pokémon has 4× its catch rate.
entries.flavorText:
A Poké Ball that will make it easier to catch
Pokémon that can evolve using a Moon Stone.

entries.name: Moon Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves Nebby into Lunala when used at the Altar of the Moone.
entries.effectText: Evolves Nebby into Lunala when used at the Altar of the Moone.
entries.flavorText:
It is said that the tones it produces were offered
up as an expression of gratitude to the Legendary
Pokémon of the moon.

entries.name: Moon Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves a Clefairy into Clefable, a Jigglypuff into Wigglytuff, a Munna into Musharna, a Nidorina into Nidoqueen, a Nidorino into Nidoking, or a Skitty into Delcatty.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves a Clefairy into Clefable, a Jigglypuff into Wigglytuff, a Munna into Musharna, a Nidorina into Nidoqueen, a Nidorino into Nidoking, or a Skitty into Delcatty.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It is as black as the night sky.

entries.name: Mosaic Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of a vivid
rainbow pattern.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Mr. Mime Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

names.ja-hrkt: ヤミカラスのおたから
names.ja: ヤミカラスのおたから

entries.name: Muscle Band
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Boosts the damage of physical moves used by the holder by 10%.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s physical moves by 10%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This headband
exudes strength, slightly boosting the power of
physical moves.

entries.name: Muscle Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases Attack effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s Attack effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the Attack base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Mystery Egg
entries.shortEffectText: Deliver to Professor Elm.
entries.effectText: Must be obtained to trigger the break-in at Professor Elm’s lab, the first rival battle, and access to Route 31.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious Egg obtained from Mr. Pokémon.
What is in the Egg is unknown.

entries.name: Mystic Water
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Water moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This
teardrop-shaped gem boosts the power of
Water-type moves.

entries.name: MysticTicket
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Navel Rock and Lugia or Ho-oh.
entries.effectText: Provides access to Navel Rock, Ho-Oh, and Lugia.
entries.flavorText:
A ticket required to board the ship
to NAVEL ROCK.
It glows with a mystic light.

entries.name: N-Lunarizer
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for n-lunarizer--merge
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for n-lunarizer--merge
entries.flavorText:
A machine to fuse Necrozma, which needs light,
and Lunala.

entries.name: N-Lunarizer
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for n-lunarizer--split
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for n-lunarizer--split
entries.flavorText:
A machine to separate Necrozma, which needed
light, from Lunala.

entries.name: N-Solarizer
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for n-solarizer--merge
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for n-solarizer--merge
entries.flavorText:
A machine to fuse Necrozma, which needs light,
and Solgaleo.

entries.name: N-Solarizer
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for n-solarizer--split
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for n-solarizer--split
entries.flavorText:
A machine to separate Necrozma, which needed
light, from Solgaleo.

entries.name: Naive Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Speed will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Def will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Nanab Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cuteness. Its pink flesh is sweet when eaten.

entries.name: Naughty Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Attack will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Def will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Nest Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3.9× for level 1 Pokémon, and drops steadily to 1× at level 30.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  Has a catch rate of given by `(40 - level) / 10`, where `level` is the wild Pokémon’s level, to a maximum of 3.9× for level 1 Pokémon.  If the wild Pokémon’s level is higher than 30, this ball has a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that becomes more
effective the lower the level of the wild Pokémon.

entries.name: Net Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3× for water and bug Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If the wild Pokémon is Water- or Bug-type, this ball has a catch rate of 3×.  Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that is more
effective when attempting to catch Water- or
Bug-type Pokémon.

entries.name: Never-Melt Ice
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ice-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ice moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a heat-
repelling piece of ice that boosts the power of
Ice-type moves.

entries.name: Nidoran♀ Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Nidoran♂ Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Nomel Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Toughness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Normal Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Normal-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Normal-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an ordinary essence. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Normal-type move
one time.

entries.name: Normalium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for normalium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for normalium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Normal-type moves to Normal-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Normalium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Normal moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Normal moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Normal-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Nugget
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 5000 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 10000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A nugget of the purest gold that gives off a lustrous
gleam in direct light. It can be sold at a high price
to shops.

entries.name: Oak’s Letter
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Seabreak path, Flower Paradise, and Shaymin.
entries.effectText: Grants access to Flower Paradise and Shaymin.
entries.flavorText:
A letter from Professor Oak. Written in it is a
request for you to go to Route 224.

entries.name: Oak’s Parcel
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Prof. Oak for a Pokédex.
entries.effectText: A parcel to be delivered to Professor Oak for a Pokédex.
entries.flavorText:
A parcel to be delivered to PROF.
OAK from VIRIDIAN CITY’s POKéMON
MART.

entries.name: Occa Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Fire-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Fire-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Fire-type attack.

entries.name: Odd Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Mr. Mime begets a Mime Jr. Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Psychic moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This
exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of
Psychic-type moves.

entries.name: Odd Keystone
entries.shortEffectText: Use on the tower on Route 209 to encounter Spiritomb if you have at least 32 Underground greetings.
entries.effectText: Place in the tower on Route 209.  Check the stone to encounter a Spiritomb, as long as the trainer’s Underground status card counts at least 32 greetings.
entries.flavorText:
A vital item that is needed to keep a stone tower
from collapsing. Voices can be heard from
it occasionally.

entries.name: Oddish Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Old Amber
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into an Aerodactyl.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive an Aerodactyl.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of amber that still contains the genetic
material of an ancient Pokémon. It’s clear with a
tawny, reddish tint.

entries.name: Old Charm
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Cynthia’s grandmother in Celestic Town for HM04 (Surf).
entries.effectText: Given to Cynthia’s grandmother to get the Surf HM.
entries.flavorText:
An ancient good-luck charm made of Pokémon
bones to be taken to the elder of Celestic Town.

entries.name: Old Gateau
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures any status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
The Old Chateau’s hidden specialty. It can be used
once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Old Letter
entries.flavorText:
A letter entrusted to you by a girl, to be delivered to
a boy. Strangely, it looks old to you.

entries.name: Old Rod
entries.shortEffectText: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
entries.effectText: Used to find Pokémon on the Old Rod list for an area, which are generally Magikarp or similar.
entries.flavorText:
An old and beat-up fishing rod. Use it at any body
of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

entries.name: Old Sea Map
entries.shortEffectText: Allows access to Faraway Island and Mew. Unreleased outside of Japan.
entries.effectText: Provides access to Faraway Island and Mew.
entries.flavorText:
A faded sea chart
that shows the way
to a certain island.

entries.name: Omanyte Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Onix Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Oran Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to recover 10 HP.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 10 HP.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 10 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can restore its own HP by 10 points
during battle.

entries.name: Orange Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
ZIGZAGOON print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Orange Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for orange-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for orange-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Ilima
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Oval Charm
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles the chance of two Pokémon producing an egg at the daycare every 255 steps.
entries.flavorText:
An oval charm said to increase the chance of
Pokémon Eggs being found at the Nursery.

entries.name: Oval Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Level-up during Day on a Happiny: Holder evolves into Chansey.
entries.effectText:
Held by Happiny
:   Holder evolves into Chansey when it levels up during the daytime.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It’s as round as a Pokémon Egg.

entries.name: Pack of Potatoes
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Potatoes help mellow out a spicy
taste, to make a dish more mild.

entries.name: Packaged Curry
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Using packaged curry helps
make sure there are no slipups when cooking.

entries.name: Pair of Tickets
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player and their mother to see the star show in the Mossdeep Space Center.
entries.effectText: Allows the player and their mother to see the star show in the Mossdeep Space Center.
entries.flavorText:
Tickets for two to the astronomical show being
held at the Mossdeep Space Center.

entries.name: Pal Pad
entries.shortEffectText: Use to record Friend Codes and check your own.
entries.effectText: Contains friend codes for up to 32 other players, as well as their sprite, gender, and basic statistics for those that have been seen on WFC.
entries.flavorText:
A convenient notepad that is used to register your
friends and friend codes and keep a record of
your gameplay.

entries.name: Pamtre Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Beauty, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Paralyze Heal
entries.shortEffectText: Cures paralysis.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures paralysis.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating paralysis. It can be
used to free a single Pokémon that has been paralyzed.

entries.name: Paras Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Parcel
entries.shortEffectText: Given to the trainer’s rival in Jubilife City. Contains Town Maps.
entries.effectText: Given to the trainer’s rival in Jubilife City.  Contains two Town Maps, one of which is given to the trainer upon delivery.
entries.flavorText:
A parcel entrusted into your care. You’re supposed
to deliver it to your childhood friend who left
Twinleaf Town.

entries.name: Parcel
entries.flavorText:
A parcel to be delivered to Professor Oak from
Viridian City’s Poké Mart.

entries.name: Park Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Catches Pokémon in the Pal Park every time.
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Catches a wild Pokémon.

This item can only be used in Pal Park.
entries.flavorText: A special Poké Ball for the Pal Park.

names.ja-hrkt: おいわいはなびピック
names.ja: おいわいはなびピック

entries.name: Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to ride the Magnet Train between Goldenrod City and Saffron City.
entries.effectText: Allows the trainer to ride the Magnet Train between Goldenrod City and Saffron City.
entries.flavorText:
A pass required for riding the Magnet Train.
It allows you to ride whenever and however much
you’d like.

entries.name: Pass Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Activates Pass Powers.
entries.effectText: Acts as currency to activate Pass Powers in the Entralink.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious orb containing the power of the
Unova region, to be used when generating
Pass Power.

entries.name: Passho Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Water-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Water-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Water-type attack.

entries.name: Pasta
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. These flour noodles make
for a surprisingly good match with curry.

entries.name: Payapa Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Psychic-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Psychic-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Psychic-type attack.

entries.name: Pearl
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 700 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 1400 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A rather small pearl that has a very nice silvery
sheen to it. It can be sold at a low price to shops.

entries.name: Pearl String
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Ore Collector for 25000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
Very large pearls that sparkle in a pretty silver
color. They can be sold at a high price to shops.

entries.name: Pecha Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when poisoned to cure poison.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is poisoned, it consumes this item to cure the poison.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures poison.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from poisoning on its own
in battle.

entries.name: Permit
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to the Nature Preserve.
entries.flavorText:
A permit that is needed to enter the
Nature Preserve. Not many know about it.

entries.name: Persim Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when confused to cure confusion.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is confused, it consumes this item to cure the confusion.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures confusion.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from confusion on its own
in battle.

entries.name: Petaya Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Special Attack.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its Special Attack by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its Sp. Atk stat will increase when it’s in
a pinch.

entries.name: Pewter Crunchies
entries.flavorText:
Pewter City’s famous crunchy snack. They can be used
to heal all status conditions of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Photo Album
entries.shortEffectText: Stores photos from your adventure.
entries.effectText: Does nothing.
entries.flavorText:
A nice photo album for storing all the photos taken
along your adventure.

entries.name: Pidgeotite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Pidgeot to Mega Evolve into Mega Pidgeot.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Pidgeot to Mega Evolve into Mega Pidgeot.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Pidgeot hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Pidgey Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Pikachu Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Pikanium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for pikanium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for pikanium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Pikachu’s Volt Tackle to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Pikanium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Pikachu to upgrade Volt Tackle into Catastropika.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Pikachu to upgrade Volt Tackle into Catastropika.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Pikachu’s Volt Tackle to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Pikashunium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for pikashunium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for pikashunium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
a Thunderbolt by Pikachu in a cap to an
exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Pikashunium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows cap-wearing Pikachu to upgrade Thunderbolt into 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows cap-wearing Pikachu to upgrade Thunderbolt into 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades a Thunderbolt by Pikachu in a cap to
a Z-Move.

entries.name: Pinap Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Toughness. Its yellow flesh is sour when eaten.

entries.name: Pink Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Love Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Love Ball.
entries.flavorText: A pink Apricorn. It has a nice, sweet scent.

entries.name: Pink Nectar
entries.shortEffectText: Changes Oricorio to Pa’u Style.
entries.effectText: Changes Oricorio to Pa’u Style.  Single-use and cannot be used in battle.
entries.flavorText:
A flower nectar obtained from the flowering shrubs
on Royal Avenue.
It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Pink Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for pink-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for pink-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Mina
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Pink Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Cuteness while in a contest.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Cuteness during a Super Contest’s Visual Competition.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest,
it raises the Cute aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

entries.name: Pinsir Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Pinsirite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Pinsir to Mega Evolve into Mega Pinsir.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Pinsir to Mega Evolve into Mega Pinsir.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Pinsir hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Pixie Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Fairy-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fairy.
entries.effectText: Held: Fairy-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Fairy.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Fairy-type moves.

names.ja-hrkt: きいろチェッククロス
names.ja: きいろチェッククロス

entries.name: Plasma Card
entries.shortEffectText: Required to progress in the Plasma Frigate.
entries.flavorText:
A card key needed to enter the password inside
the Plasma Frigate.

entries.name: Plume Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into an Archen.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in a museum to receive an Archen.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sky. It looks as if it could come from part of
its wing.

entries.name: Poffin Case
entries.shortEffectText: Holds Poffins.
entries.effectText: Contains up to 100 Poffins.
entries.flavorText: A case for storing Poffins cooked from Berries.

entries.name: Point Card
entries.shortEffectText: Keeps count of Battle Points earned.
entries.effectText: Tracks Battle Points.
entries.flavorText:
A card that lists how many Battle Points you
have earned.

entries.name: Poison Barb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Poison-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Poison moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This small,
poisonous barb boosts the power of
Poison-type moves.

entries.name: Poison Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Poison-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Poison-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of poison. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Poison-type move
one time.

entries.name: Poison Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Poison form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Poison.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Poison form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Poison.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Poison-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Poisonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for poisonium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for poisonium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Poison-type moves to Poison-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Poisonium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Poison moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Poison moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Poison-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Poké Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A device for catching wild Pokémon. It’s thrown like
a ball at a Pokémon, comfortably encapsulating
its target.

entries.name: Poké Doll
entries.shortEffectText: Ends a wild battle.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Ends a wild battle.  Cannot be used in trainer battles.
entries.flavorText:
A doll that attracts the attention of a Pokémon.
It guarantees escape from any battle with
wild Pokémon.

entries.name: Poké Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Use to awaken sleeping Pokémon, including Snorlax on roads.
entries.effectText: Wakes up sleeping Pokémon.  Required to wake up sleeping Snorlax on the overworld.
entries.flavorText:
A flute that can play with such a beautiful tone that
even sleeping Pokémon find themselves woken up.

entries.name: Poké Radar
entries.shortEffectText: Use to track down rare or shiny Pokémon. 50 steps to recharge.
entries.effectText: Designates several nearby patches of grass as containing Pokémon, some of which may be special radar-only Pokémon.  Successive uses in a certain way create chains of encounters with the same species; longer chains increase the chance that a shiny Pokémon of that species will appear.
entries.flavorText:
A tool that can search out Pokémon that are hiding
in the tall grass. Its battery is recharged as
you walk.

entries.name: Poké Toy
entries.shortEffectText: Ends a wild battle.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Ends a wild battle.  Cannot be used in trainer battles.
entries.flavorText:
A toy that attracts the attention of a Pokémon.
It guarantees escape from any battle with
wild Pokémon.

entries.name: Pokéblock Case
entries.shortEffectText: Holds Pokéblocks.
entries.effectText: Stores Pokéblocks.
entries.flavorText:
A case for holding POKéBLOCKS made
with a BERRY BLENDER. It releases
one POKéBLOCK when shaken.

entries.name: Pokéblock Kit
entries.shortEffectText: Creates and stores Pokéblocks.
entries.effectText: Creates and stores Pokéblocks.
entries.flavorText:
A set containing a Berry Blender for making
Pokéblocks and a Pokéblock Case for
storing Pokéblocks.

entries.name: Pokémon Box Link
entries.flavorText:
A device that allows you to access the Pokémon storage
system. There are some places where it won’t work.

entries.name: Polished Mud Ball
entries.flavorText:
A ball made of mud. It had been polished for such
a long time that the surface is very shiny.

entries.name: Poliwag Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Pomeg Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops HP Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers HP effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
HP base points.

entries.name: Ponyta Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Porygon Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Potion
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 20 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a friendly Pokémon
:   Restores 20 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can
be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Powder Jar
entries.shortEffectText: Stores Berry Powder made using a Berry Crusher.
entries.effectText: Holds Berry Powder from Berry Crushing.
entries.flavorText:
A jar for storing BERRY POWDER
made using a BERRY CRUSHER.

entries.name: Power Anklet
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 Speed effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains Speed effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed
but allows the holder’s Speed stat to grow more
after battling.

entries.name: Power Band
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 Special Defense effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains Special Defense effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed
but allows the holder’s Sp. Def stat to grow more
after battling.

entries.name: Power Belt
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 Defense effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains Defense effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed
but allows the holder’s Defense stat to grow more
after battling.

entries.name: Power Bracer
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 Attack effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains Attack effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed
but allows the holder’s Attack stat to grow more
after battling.

entries.name: Power Herb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Both turns of a two-turn charge move happen at once. Consumed upon use.
entries.effectText: Held: Whenever the holder uses a move that requires a turn to charge first (Bounce, Dig, Dive, Fly, Razor Wind, Skull Bash, Sky Attack, or Solarbeam), this item is consumed and the charge is skipped.  Skull Bash still provides a Defense boost.
entries.flavorText:
A single-use item to be held by a Pokémon. It
allows the holder to immediately use a move that
normally requires a turn to charge.

entries.name: Power Lens
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 Special Attack effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains Special Attack effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed
but allows the holder’s Sp. Atk stat to grow more
after battling.

entries.name: Power Plant Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Permits entry to the Kalos Power Plant.
entries.effectText: Permits entry to the Kalos Power Plant.
entries.flavorText:
This pass serves as an ID card for gaining access
to the Power Plant that lies along Route 13.

entries.name: Power Weight
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder gains 4 HP effort values, but has halved Speed in battle.
entries.effectText: Held: Decreases the holder’s Speed by 50%.  Whenever the holder gains HP effort from battle, increases that effort by 4; this applies before the PokéRUS doubling effect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It reduces Speed but
allows the holder’s max HP to grow more after battling.

entries.name: PP Max
entries.shortEffectText: Raises a move’s max PP by 60%.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases a selected move’s max PP to 1.6× its original max PP.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A medicine that optimally raises the max PP of a single
selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

entries.name: PP Up
entries.shortEffectText: Raises a move’s max PP by 20%.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases a selected move’s max PP by 20% its original max PP, to a maximum of 1.6×.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A medicine that slightly raises the max PP of a single
selected move that has been learned by a Pokémon.

entries.name: Precooked Burger
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. If you’re unsure about what to
cook, you can’t go wrong with some burger.

entries.name: Premier Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat rare Poké Ball that was made as a
commemorative item used to celebrate an event of
some sort.

entries.name: Pretty Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 100 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
Though this feather is beautiful, it’s just a regular
feather and has no effect.

entries.name: Primarium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for primarium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for primarium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Primarina’s Sparkling Aria to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Primarium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Primarina to upgrade Sparkling Aria into Oceanic Operetta.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Primarina to upgrade Sparkling Aria into Oceanic Operetta.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Primarina’s Sparkling Aria to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Prism Scale
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Feebas: Holder evolves into Milotic.
entries.effectText:
Held by Feebas
:   Holder evolves into Milotic when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious scale that causes a certain Pokémon
to evolve. It shines in rainbow colors.

entries.name: Prison Bottle
entries.shortEffectText: Transforms Hoopa into its Unbound form for up to three days.
entries.effectText: Transforms Hoopa into its Unbound form for up to three days.
entries.flavorText:
A bottle believed to have been used to seal away
the power of a certain Pokémon long, long ago.

entries.name: Professor’s Mask
entries.shortEffectText: Lost by Professor Kukui.
entries.effectText: Lost by Professor Kukui.
entries.flavorText:
The mask that belongs to The Masked Royal.
This pro wrestler apparently sews his mask on a
machine himself.

entries.name: Prof’s Letter
entries.shortEffectText: Traded to the player’s mom for the Town Map and a Potion.
entries.effectText: Traded to the player’s mom for the Town Map and a Potion.
entries.flavorText:
A letter that Professor Sycamore wrote to your
mother. A faint but pleasant perfume seems to
cling to the paper.

entries.name: Prop Case
entries.shortEffectText: Stores props for the Pokémon Musical.
entries.effectText: Stores props for the Pokémon Musical.
entries.flavorText:
A lovely case to store colorful Props for your
Pokémon to wear in a musical.

entries.name: Protective Pads
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Prevents side effects of contact moves used on the holder.
entries.effectText: Held: Prevents side effects of contact moves used on the holder.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. These pads
protect the holder from effects caused by
making direct contact with the target.

entries.name: Protector
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Rhydon: Holder evolves into Rhyperior.
entries.effectText: Held by Rhydon: Evolves the holder into Rhyperior when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A protective item of some sort. It is extremely stiff
and heavy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Protein
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Attack effort and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases Attack effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed,
it raises the Attack base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Psychic Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Psychic-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Psychic-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of the mind. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Psychic-type move
one time.

entries.name: Psychic Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Psychic form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Psychic.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Psychic form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Psychic.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Psychic-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Psychic Seed
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed on Psychic Terrain and raises the holder’s Special Defense by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder enters battle during Psychic Terrain, or if Psychic Terrain is activated while the holder is in battle, this item is consumed and the holder’s Special Defense raises by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Sp. Def
on Psychic Terrain. It can only be used once.

entries.name: Psychium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for psychium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for psychium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Psychic-type moves to Psychic-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Psychium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Psychic moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Psychic moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Psychic-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Psyduck Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Pungent Root
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. Though carelessly picked, these
herbs can add a fragrant accent to a dish.

entries.name: Pure Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon. Breeding: Chimecho begets a Chingling Egg.
entries.effectText: Held by lead Pokémon: Prevents wild battles with Pokémon that are lower level than the holder.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep
wild Pokémon away if the holder is the head of
the party.

entries.name: Purple Nectar
entries.shortEffectText: Changes Oricorio to Sensu Style.
entries.effectText: Changes Oricorio to Sensu Style.  Single-use and cannot be used in battle.
entries.flavorText:
A flower nectar obtained at Poni Meadow.
It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Purple Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for purple-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for purple-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Nanu
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Qualot Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops Defense Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers Defense effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
Defense base points.

entries.name: Quick Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate is 4× (Gen V: 5×), but only on the first turn.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 4× on the first turn of a battle, but 1× any other time.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that has a more
successful catch rate if used at the start of a
wild encounter.

entries.name: Quick Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Speed stat.

entries.name: Quick Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Speed stat.

entries.name: Quick Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Speed stat.

entries.name: Quick Claw
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder has a 3/16 (18.75%) chance to move first.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   Whenever the holder attempts to use a move, it has a 3/16 chance to act first among moves with the same priority.  If multiple Pokémon have this effect at the same time, Speed is the tie-breaker as normal, but the effect of Trick Room is ignored.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This light, sharp
claw lets the bearer move first occasionally.

entries.name: Quick Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles Ditto’s Speed when held. The boost is lost after transforming.
entries.effectText: Held by Ditto: Doubles the holder’s initial Speed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Ditto. Extremely fine yet
hard, this odd powder boosts the Speed stat.

entries.name: Quiet Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Atk will grow
more easily, but its Speed will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Rabuta Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cleverness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Radiant Petal
entries.flavorText:
A single petal that the King of Bountiful Harvests
gave you. It radiates faint light.

entries.name: Rage Candy Bar
entries.shortEffectText: HS: Traded for TM64. Gen II & Gen V: Acts as a Potion.
entries.effectText: May be traded for a TM64 in the vertical Underground Path.
entries.flavorText:
Mahogany Town’s famous candy. It can be used
once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Rainbow Flower
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for rainbow-flower
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for rainbow-flower
entries.flavorText:
A flower made of the petals you received from
captains. It’s proof that your growth has been
recognized by them.

entries.name: Rainbow Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to all of the Sevii Islands.
entries.effectText: Provides access to the Sevii Islands.
entries.flavorText:
A pass for ferries between
VERMILION and the SEVII ISLANDS.
It features a drawing of a rainbow.

entries.name: Rainbow Wing
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Ho-Oh at the top of the Bell Tower.
entries.effectText: Causes Ho-Oh to appear at the top of Bell Tower.
entries.flavorText: A mystical rainbow feather that sparkles.

names.ja-hrkt: ラルトスのおとしもの
names.ja: ラルトスのおとしもの

entries.name: Rare Bone
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 5000 Pokédollars, or to Bone Man for 10000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A rare bone that is extremely valuable for the
study of Pokémon archeology. It can be sold for a
high price to shops.

entries.name: Rare Candy
