entries.shortEffectText: Causes a level-up and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases level by 1.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed,
it will instantly raise the level of a single Pokémon
by one.

entries.name: Rash Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Atk will grow
more easily, but its Sp. Def will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Rattata Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Rawst Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when burned to cure a burn.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder is burned, it consumes this item to cure the burn.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Cures a burn.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can recover from a burn on its own
in battle.

entries.name: Razor Claw
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit ratio by one stage. Held by a Sneasel while levelling up at night: Holder evolves into Weavile.
entries.effectText:
Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit counter by 1.
Held by Sneasel: Evolves the holder into Weavile when it levels up during the night.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This sharply
hooked claw increases the holder’s
critical-hit ratio.

entries.name: Razor Fang
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Damaging moves gain a 10% chance to make their target flinch. Held by a Gligar while levelling up: Holder evolves into Gliscor.
entries.effectText:
Held: When the holder attacks with most damaging moves, provides an extra 11.7% (30/256) chance for the target to flinch.
Held by Gligar: Evolves the holder into Gliscor when it levels up.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder
successfully inflicts damage, the target may
also flinch.

entries.name: Razz Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Coolness. Its red flesh is spicy when eaten.

entries.name: Reaper Cloth
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Dusclops: Holder evolves into Dusknoir.
entries.effectText: Held by Dusclops: Evolves the holder into Dusknoir when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A cloth imbued with horrifyingly strong spiritual
energy. It’s loved by a certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Red Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Level Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Level Ball.
entries.flavorText: A red Apricorn. It assails your nostrils.

names.ja-hrkt: あかパプリカスライス
names.ja: あかパプリカスライス

entries.name: Red Card
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder takes damage from a move, the opponent switches out for another random party Pokémon. Consumed after use.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder takes damage directly from a move and does not faint, it switches out for another random, non-fainted Pokémon in its party.
This effect does not activate if another effect prevents the holder from switching out.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder
is hit by an attack, the attacker is removed
from battle.

entries.name: Red Chain
entries.shortEffectText: Used to bind Palkia and Dialga.
entries.effectText: Unused.
entries.flavorText:
A mythical chain that is said to link the
Legendary Pokémon that created the Sinnoh region.

entries.name: Red Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Cures attraction.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Cures attraction.
entries.flavorText:
A flute made from red glass. It snaps Pokémon
out of infatuation.

entries.name: Red Nectar
entries.shortEffectText: Changes Oricorio to Baile Style.
entries.effectText: Changes Oricorio to Baile Style.  Single-use and cannot be used in battle.
entries.flavorText:
A flower nectar obtained at Ula’ula Meadow.
It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Red Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Groudon to the Embedded Tower.
entries.effectText: Causes Groudon to appear in the Embedded Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A shiny red orb that is said to have a legend tied
to it. It’s known to have a deep connection with the
Hoenn region.

entries.name: Red Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for red-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for red-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Kiawe
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Red Scale
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Mr. Pokémon for an Exp. Share.
entries.effectText: May be traded to Mr. Pokémon for an Exp. Share.
entries.flavorText:
A scale from a red Gyarados. It glows red like
a flame.

entries.name: Red Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Coolness while in a contest.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Coolness during a Super Contest’s Visual Competition.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest,
it raises the Cool aspect of the Pokémon holding it.

entries.name: Red Shard
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
entries.effectText:
No effect.

In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a Sunny Day TM in the house midway along the southern section of Route 212.

In Platinum, trade to move tutors on Route 212, in Snowpoint City, and in the Survival Area.  Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Cheri Berry, a Leppa Berry, and a Pecha Berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in Violet City.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Persim Berry, a Pomeg Berry, and a Razz Berry with the Juggler near the Pal Park entrance in Fuchsia City.
entries.flavorText:
A small red shard. It appears to be a fragment of
some sort of implement made long ago.

entries.name: Reins of Unity
entries.flavorText:
Cords that shine when you hold them up to the light.
The people of old times presented these to the King of
Bountiful Harvests to express their gratitude.

entries.name: Reins of Unity
entries.flavorText:
Reins that people presented to the king. They enhance
Calyrex’s power over bountiful harvests and unite
Calyrex with its beloved steed.

entries.name: Reins of Unity
entries.flavorText:
Reins that people presented to the king. They enable
those who are connected to communicate with each
other. The reins separate Calyrex and its beloved steed.

entries.name: Relaxed Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Defense will grow
more easily, but its Speed will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Relic Band
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner for 100000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A heavy bracelet made by an ancient civilization
about three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Copper
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner for 1000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A copper coin used by an ancient civilization about
three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Crown
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner for 300000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A heavy crown made by an ancient civilization
about three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Gold
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner 10000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A gold coin used by an ancient civilization about
three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Silver
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner 5000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A silver coin used by an ancient civilization about
three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Statue
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner 200000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A stone figurine made by an ancient civilization
about three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Relic Vase
entries.shortEffectText: Sell to Villa Owner 50000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Cult vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A fragile vase made by an ancient civilization about
three thousand years ago.

entries.name: Repeat Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 3× for previously-caught Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  If the wild Pokémon’s species is marked as caught in the trainer’s Pokédex, this ball has a catch rate of 3×.  Otherwise, it has a catch rate of 1×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that works
especially well on a Pokémon species that has been
caught before.

entries.name: Repel
entries.shortEffectText: For 100 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon.  This effect wears off after the trainer takes 100 steps.
entries.flavorText:
An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon
from jumping out at you for a while.

entries.name: Reply Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to make it easy to respond
to a letter you received. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Reset Urge
entries.shortEffectText: Resets a friendly Pokémon’s stat changes.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Selects another friendly Pokémon at random.  Removes all of that Pokémon’s stat changes.

This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
When used, it restores any stat changes of an
ally Pokémon.

entries.name: Resist Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases Defense effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s Defense effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the Defense base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Retro Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a print
of three cute POKéMON.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Reveal Glass
entries.shortEffectText: Switches Tornadus, Thundurus, and Landorus between Incarnate and Therian Forme.
entries.flavorText:
A looking glass that reveals the truth. It’s a
mysterious glass that returns a Pokémon to its
original shape.

entries.name: Revival Herb
entries.shortEffectText: Revives with full HP, but lowers happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Revives a fainted Pokémon and restores its HP to full.  Decreases happiness by 10/10/15.
entries.flavorText:
A terribly bitter medicinal herb. It revives a fainted
Pokémon and fully restores its max HP.

entries.name: Revive
entries.shortEffectText: Revives with half HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Revives the Pokémon and restores half its HP.
entries.flavorText:
A medicine that can be used to revive a single
Pokémon that has fainted. It also restores half of
the Pokémon’s max HP.

entries.name: Rhyhorn Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Ribbon Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A ribbon-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Rich Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Increases the total number of Berries by 2.
entries.effectText: Increases the total number of Berries by 2.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Ride Pager
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to summon a Ride Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to summon a Ride Pokémon.  Unused, as this can be done simply by pressing Y.
entries.flavorText:
By entering certain numbers on this pager,
you can summon Ride Pokémon in an instant.

entries.name: Rindo Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Grass-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Grass-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Grass-type attack.

entries.name: Ring Target
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Negates the holder’s type immunities. Ability immunities are not removed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When one of the user’s types would render it immune to damage, that type is ignored for damage calculation.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. Moves that
normally have no effect will land on a Pokémon
holding it.

entries.name: Rm. 1 Key
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks room 1 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.effectText: Unlocks room 1 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens the door to Room
1 inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
It is old and looks easily broken.

entries.name: Rm. 2 Key
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks room 2 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.effectText: Unlocks room 2 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens the door to Room
2 inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
It is old and looks easily broken.

entries.name: Rm. 4 Key
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks room 4 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.effectText: Unlocks room 4 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens the door to Room
4 inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
It is old and looks easily broken.

entries.name: Rm. 6 Key
entries.shortEffectText: Unlocks room 6 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.effectText: Unlocks room 6 on the Abandoned Ship.
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens the door to Room
6 inside the ABANDONED SHIP.
It is old and looks easily broken.

entries.name: Rock Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Rock-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Rock-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of rock. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Rock-type move
one time.

entries.name: Rock Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Rock-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Sudowoodo begets a Bonsly Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Rock moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This
exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of
Rock-type moves.

entries.name: Rock Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Rock form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Rock.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Rock form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Rock.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Rock-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Rockium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for rockium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for rockium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Rock-type moves to Rock-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Rockium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Rock moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Rock moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Rock-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Rocky Helmet
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/6 its max HP in damage.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/6 its max HP in damage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder is
hit, the attacker will also be damaged upon contact.

entries.name: Roller Skates
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player to move quickly and off the grid.
entries.effectText: Allows the player to move quickly and off the grid.
entries.flavorText:
Attaches roller skates to the bottom of your shoes,
allowing you to glide quickly around and
perform tricks.

entries.name: Room Service
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. Lowers Speed when
Trick Room takes effect.

entries.name: Root Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Lileep.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive a Lileep.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
in the sea. It looks as if it could be part of a
plant’s root.

entries.name: Rose Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Grass-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Roselia or Roserade beget a Budew Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Grass moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This
exotic-smelling incense boosts the power of
Grass-type moves.

entries.name: Roseli Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Fairy-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Fairy-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Fairy-type attack.

entries.name: Roto Bargain
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-bargain
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-bargain
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It reduces the prices of products at Poké Marts
by half.

entries.name: Roto Boost
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-boost
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-boost
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It raises all stats of your battling Pokémon.

entries.name: Roto Catch
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-catch
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-catch
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It increases the chance to catch Pokémon
a lot.

entries.name: Roto Encounter
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-encounter
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-encounter
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It increases the chance of encountering high-level
wild Pokémon a lot for a certain period of time.

entries.name: Roto Exp. Points
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-exp-points
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-exp-points
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It increases the Exp. Points your Pokémon receive
after battle a little.

entries.name: Roto Friendship
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-friendship
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-friendship
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It helps Pokémon in your party grow friendly faster.

entries.name: Roto Hatch
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-hatch
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-hatch
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It helps Eggs hatch faster.

entries.name: Roto HP Restore
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-hp-restore
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-hp-restore
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It fully restores the HP of your battling Pokémon.

entries.name: Roto PP Restore
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-pp-restore
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-pp-restore
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It fully restores the PP of your battling Pokémon.

entries.name: Roto Prize Money
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-prize-money
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-prize-money
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It triples the prize money you receive after battle.

entries.name: Roto Stealth
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for roto-stealth
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for roto-stealth
entries.flavorText:
One of the Rotom Powers.
It prevents you from encountering wild Pokémon for
a certain period of time.

entries.name: Rotom Bike
entries.flavorText:
This bike can be combined with the Pokémon Rotom to
use a turbo boost.

entries.name: Rotom Bike
entries.flavorText:
This bike can do a turbo boost and run on the water
using Rotom’s power. It’s a glistening black that
shines reddish-black.

entries.name: Rotom Bike
entries.flavorText:
This bike can do a turbo boost and run on the water
using Rotom’s power. It’s a sparkling white that
shines brilliantly.

entries.name: Rotom Bike
entries.flavorText:
This bike can be combined with the Pokémon Rotom to
not only do a turbo boost but also run on the water.

entries.name: Rotom Catalog
entries.flavorText:
A catalog of devices that Rotom like. Use the catalog
to have Rotom hop in and out of the various devices
listed within.

entries.name: Rowap Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed to deal 1/8 attacker’s max HP when holder is struck by a special attack.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder takes special damage, it consumes this item to damage the attacking Pokémon for 1/8 its max HP.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a special
attack hits the Pokémon holding it, the attacker will
also be hurt.

entries.name: RSVP Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to allow you to extend an
invitation to the recipient. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Ruby
entries.shortEffectText: Give to Celio with Sapphire to activate Network Machine and get Rainbow Pass.
entries.effectText: Deliver to Celio for use in the Network Machine.
entries.flavorText:
An exquisitely beautiful gem that
has a red glow.
It symbolizes passion.

entries.name: Rule Book
entries.shortEffectText: List of battle types and their rules.
entries.effectText: Unused.
entries.flavorText:
It lists the rules for holding battles.
For Link Battles, you may choose which set of rules
you wish to use.

entries.name: Rusted Shield
entries.flavorText:
It is said that a hero used this shield to halt a terrible
disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

entries.name: Rusted Sword
entries.flavorText:
It is said that a hero used this sword to halt a terrible
disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

entries.name: Sablenite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Sableye to Mega Evolve into Mega Sableye.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Sableye to Mega Evolve into Mega Sableye.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Sableye hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Sachet
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Spritzee: Holder evolves into Aromatisse.
entries.effectText: Traded on a Spritzee: Holder evolves into Aromatisse.
entries.flavorText:
A sachet filled with fragrant perfumes that are just
slightly too overwhelming. Yet it’s loved by a
certain Pokémon.

entries.name: Sacred Ash
entries.shortEffectText: Revives all fainted Pokémon with full HP.
entries.effectText:
Used
:   Revives all fainted Pokémon in the party and restores their HP to full.
entries.flavorText:
This rare ash can revive all fainted Pokémon in a
party. In doing so, it also fully restores their max HP.

entries.name: Safari Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon in the Great Marsh or Safari Zone.  Success rate is 1.5×.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 1.5×.

This item can only be used in the Great Marsh or Safari Zone.
entries.flavorText:
A special Poké Ball that was used in the Safari Zone
in the Kanto region and in the Great Marsh in the
Sinnoh region.

entries.name: Safety Goggles
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Prevents damage from powder moves and the damage from Hail and Sandstorm.
entries.effectText: Held: Prevents damage from powder moves and the damage from Hail and Sandstorm.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. These goggles
protect the holder from both weather-related
damage and powder.

entries.name: Sail Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into an Amaura.
entries.effectText: Can be revived into an Amaura.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
on the land. It looks like the impression from a
skin sail.

entries.name: Salac Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost Speed.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise its Speed by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, its Speed stat will increase when it’s in
a pinch.

entries.name: Salad Mix
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. The assortment of vegetables
looks quite appealing and healthy.

entries.name: Salamencite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Salamence to Mega Evolve into Mega Salamence.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Salamence to Mega Evolve into Mega Salamence.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Salamence hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Sandshrew Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Sapphire
entries.shortEffectText: Give to Celio with Ruby to activate Network Machine and get Rainbow Pass.
entries.effectText: Deliver to Celio for use in the Network Machine.
entries.flavorText:
An exquisitely beautiful gem that
has a blue glow.
It symbolizes honesty.

entries.name: Sassy Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Sp. Def will grow
more easily, but its Speed will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Sausages
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. These sausages should be
boiled before eating.

entries.name: Scanner
entries.shortEffectText: Trade to Captain Stern for a DeepSeaTooth or DeepSeaScale.
entries.effectText: May be traded to Captain Stern for a Deep Sea Tooth or a Deep Sea Scale.
entries.flavorText: A device found inside Sea Mauville.

entries.name: Sceptilite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Sceptile to Mega Evolve into Mega Sceptile.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Sceptile to Mega Evolve into Mega Sceptile.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Sceptile hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Scizorite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Scizor to Mega Evolve into Mega Scizor.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Scizor to Mega Evolve into Mega Scizor.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Scizor hold it, and this stone will enable it to
Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Scope Lens
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit ratio by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: Raises the holder’s critical hit counter by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a lens for
scoping out weak points. It boosts the holder’s
critical-hit ratio.

entries.name: Scyther Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Sea Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Marill or Azumarill beget an Azurill Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Water moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This incense has
a curious aroma that boosts the power of
Water-type moves.

entries.name: Seal Bag
entries.shortEffectText: Holds ten Seals for Poké Balls.
entries.effectText: Unused.
entries.flavorText:
A tiny bag that can hold 10 Seals for decorating
Poké Balls.

entries.name: Seal Case
entries.shortEffectText: Stores Seals that can be applied to Poké Ball capsules.
entries.effectText: Contains Seals used for decorating Pokéballs.
entries.flavorText:
A case for storing Seals that can be applied to the
exteriors of Poké Balls.

entries.name: Secret Key
entries.shortEffectText: Gen IV: The key to Rotom’s appliance room. Gen III: The key to Cinnabar Gym.
entries.effectText:
Used by trainer in the Galactic Eterna Building, on the ground floor, to the left of the TV
:   Unlocks the secret Rotom room, in which there are five appliances which can change Rotom’s form.
entries.flavorText:
A high-tech key that has to be used at a specific
location. It emits a special electronic signal to open
a door.

entries.name: Secret Key
entries.flavorText:
The key that unlocks the front door of
the Pokémon Gym on Cinnabar Island.
It has a red decoration.

entries.name: Secret Potion
entries.shortEffectText: Used to heal the Ampharos at the top of Olivine Lighthouse.
entries.effectText: Required to cure the Psyducks blocking Route 210 of their chronic headaches.
entries.flavorText:
A fantastic medicine dispensed by the pharmacy in
Cianwood City. It fully heals a Pokémon of
any ailment.

entries.name: Seel Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Serious Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, all of its stats will
grow at an equal rate.

entries.name: Shaderoot Carrot
entries.flavorText:
Produce that the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved
steed likes to eat. It grew in a gloomy field.

entries.name: Shadow Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
DUSKULL print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Shalour Sable
entries.shortEffectText: Cures any major status ailment and confusion.
entries.effectText: Cures any major status ailment and confusion.
entries.flavorText:
Shalour City’s famous shortbread. It can be used
once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon.

entries.name: Sharp Beak
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Flying-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Flying moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a long, sharp
beak that boosts the power of Flying-type moves.

entries.name: Sharpedonite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Sharpedo to Mega Evolve into Mega Sharpedo.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Sharpedo to Mega Evolve into Mega Sharpedo.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Sharpedo hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Shed Shell
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder can bypass all trapping effects and switch out. Multi-turn moves still cannot be switched out of.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder is unaffected by any moves or abilities that would prevent it from actively leaving battle.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This discarded
carapace enables the holder to switch out of battle
without fail.

entries.name: Shell Bell
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder receives 1/8 of the damage it deals when attacking.
entries.effectText: Held: Heals the holder by 1/8 of any damage it inflicts.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The holder
restores a little HP every time it inflicts damage
on others.

entries.name: Shellder Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

names.ja-hrkt: シェルダーのしんじゅ
names.ja: シェルダーのしんじゅ

names.ja-hrkt: カラナクシのねんえき
names.ja: カラナクシのねんえき

entries.name: Shiny Charm
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the chance of finding a shiny Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A shiny charm said to increase the chance of
finding a Shiny Pokémon in the wild.

entries.name: Shiny Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves a Minccino into Cinccino, a Roselia into Roserade, or a Togetic into Togekiss.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves a Minccino into Cinccino, a Roselia into Roserade, or a Togetic into Togekiss.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It shines with a dazzling light.

entries.name: Shoal Salt
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Gen III: Trade four and four Shoal Shells for a Shell Bell.
entries.effectText: No effect.
entries.flavorText: Salt that you found inside Shoal Cave.

entries.name: Shoal Shell
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Gen III: Trade four and four Shoal Salts for a Shell Bell.
entries.effectText: No effect.
entries.flavorText: A seashell that you found inside Shoal Cave.

entries.name: Shock Drive
entries.shortEffectText: Grants Genesect a yellow, Electric-type Techno Blast.
entries.effectText:
Held by Genesect
:   Holder’s buster is yellow, and its Techno Blast is Electric-type.
entries.flavorText:
A cassette to be held by Genesect. It changes
Genesect’s Techno Blast move so it becomes
Electric type.

names.ja-hrkt: シルシュルーのインク
names.ja: シルシュルーのインク

entries.name: Shuca Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Ground-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Ground-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Ground-type attack.

names.ja-hrkt: カゲボウズのきれはし
names.ja: カゲボウズのきれはし

entries.name: Silk Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Normal-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Normal moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a sumptuous
scarf that boosts the power of Normal-type moves.

entries.name: Silph Scope
entries.shortEffectText: Used to identify the true forms of ghosts in Pokémon Tower.
entries.effectText: Identifies ghosts in Pokémon Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A scope that lets you see what cannot usually be seen.
It is made by Silph Co.

names.ja-hrkt: シルバーチタンボトル
names.ja: シルバーチタンボトル

names.ja-hrkt: シルバーチタンカップ
names.ja: シルバーチタンカップ

entries.name: Silver Leaf
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious silver leaf. No trees with this leaf have
been found yet.

entries.name: Silver Nanab Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch
when given to them.

entries.name: Silver Pinap Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that makes you more likely to get an item when
given to Pokémon you’re trying to catch.

entries.name: Silver Powder
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Bug-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   Holder’s Bug-type moves have 1.2× their power.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a shiny
silver powder that will boost the power of
Bug-type moves.

entries.name: Silver Razz Berry
entries.flavorText:
A Berry that makes it easier to catch Pokémon when
given to them.

entries.name: Silver Wing
entries.shortEffectText: Summons Lugia to the Whirl Islands.
entries.effectText: Causes Lugia to appear in the Whirl Islands.
entries.flavorText: A strange, silvery feather that sparkles.

entries.name: Sitrus Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to recover 1/4 max HP.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/4 its max HP.

Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 1/4 the Pokémon’s max HP.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, it can restore its own HP by a small
amount during battle.

entries.name: Skull Fossil
entries.shortEffectText: Can be revived into a Cranidos.
entries.effectText: Give to a scientist in the Mining Museum in Oreburgh City or the Museum of Science in Pewter City to receive a Cranidos.
entries.flavorText:
A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived
on the land. It appears as though it’s part of
a head.

entries.name: Sky Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Flying-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Flying.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Flying moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Flying.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Flying-type moves.

entries.name: Slowbronite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Slowbro to Mega Evolve into Mega Slowbro.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Slowbro to Mega Evolve into Mega Slowbro.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Slowbro hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Slowpoke Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Slowpoke Tail
entries.shortEffectText: A tasty tail that sells for a high price.
entries.effectText: Does nothing.
entries.flavorText:
A very tasty tail of something. It can be sold at a
high price to shops.

names.ja-hrkt: マグマッグのようがん
names.ja: マグマッグのようがん

entries.name: Small Bouquet
entries.flavorText:
A small bouquet that was made with heart and soul
for a single Trainer.

entries.name: Smart Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: Smart Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: Smart Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase that
Pokémon’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: Smoke Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows the Holder to escape from any wild battle.
entries.effectText: Held: In wild battles, attempts to run away on the holder’s turn will always succeed.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It enables the
holder to flee from any wild Pokémon encounter
without fail.

entries.name: Smoke-Poke Tail
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. When a Slowpoke’s tail falls off,
it grows back quickly.

entries.name: Smooth Rock
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Sandstorm by the holder lasts 8 rounds instead of 5.
entries.effectText: Held: The holder’s Sandstorm will create a sandstorm lasting for eight turns rather than five.  As this item affects the move rather than the weather itself, the effect is not lost if the holder leaves battle or drops this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of the move Sandstorm when used by
the holder.

entries.name: Snorlax Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Snorlium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for snorlium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for snorlium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Snorlax’s Giga Impact to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Snorlium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Snorlax to upgrade Giga Impact into Pulverizing Pancake.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Snorlax to upgrade Giga Impact into Pulverizing Pancake.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Snorlax’s Giga Impact to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Snow Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of a
chilly, snow-covered world.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Snowball
entries.shortEffectText: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Ice move, raises its Attack by one stage.
entries.effectText: Held: If the holder is hit by a damaging Ice move, raises its Attack by one stage.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack
if the holder is hit with an Ice-type attack. It can only
be used once.

entries.name: Soda Pop
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 60 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 60 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A highly carbonated soda drink. It can be used to
restore 50 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Soft Sand
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ground-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ground moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a loose, silky
sand that boosts the power of Ground-type moves.

entries.name: Solganium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for solganium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for solganium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Solgaleo’s Sunsteel Strike to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Solganium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for solganium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for solganium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Solgaleo’s Sunsteel Strike to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Sonia’s Book
entries.flavorText:
Professor Sonia’s published writings. Her new
discoveries about the Galar region’s legends are
recorded in this enjoyable read.

entries.name: Soot Sack
entries.shortEffectText: Stores volcanic ash from Route 113.
entries.effectText: Collects soot when walking through tall grass on Route 113.
entries.flavorText: A sack used to gather and hold volcanic ash.

entries.name: Soothe Bell
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Doubles the happiness earned by the holder.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder would earn happiness for any reason, it earns twice that amount instead.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. The comforting
chime of this bell calms the holder, making
it friendly.

entries.name: Soul Dew
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Latias and Latios’s Special Attack and Special Defense by 50%.
entries.effectText: Held by Latias or Latios: Increases the holder’s Special Attack and Special Defense by 50%.
entries.flavorText:
A wondrous orb to be held by either Latios or
Latias. It raises the power of Psychic- and
Dragon-type moves.

entries.name: Space Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print depicting
the huge expanse of space.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Sparkling Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Required to obtain a Z-Ring.
entries.effectText: Required to obtain a Z-Ring.
entries.flavorText:
A stone entrusted by a Pokémon that has been
venerated as a guardian deity in the Alola region.
There is said to be some secret in how it sparkles.

entries.name: Spearow Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Spell Tag
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ghost-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ghost moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a sinister,
eerie tag that boosts the power of
Ghost-type moves.

entries.name: Spelon Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cuteness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Spice Mix
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. There are over fifty spices
contained within this ingredient’s hot flavor.

entries.name: Splash Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Water.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Water moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Water.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

entries.name: Spooky Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Ghost-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Ghost.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Ghost moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Ghost.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Ghost-type moves.

entries.name: Sport Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a Pokémon in the Bug-Catching contest in National Park. (Gen II: Park Ball)
entries.effectText:
Used by a trainer in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.

    The wild Pokémon’s catch rate is 1.5× normal.
entries.flavorText:
A special Poké Ball that was used during the
Bug-Catching Contest in the Johto region.

entries.name: Sprayduck
entries.shortEffectText: Used to water berries.
entries.effectText: Waters Berry plants.
entries.flavorText:
A watering can shaped like a Psyduck. It helps
promote the healthy growth of any Berries planted
in good, soft soil.

entries.name: Sprinklotad
entries.shortEffectText: Waters Berry plants.
entries.effectText: Waters Berry plants.
entries.flavorText:
A watering can shaped like a Lotad. It helps
promote the healthy growth of any Berries planted
in good, soft soil.

entries.name: Squirt Bottle
entries.shortEffectText: Use on Sudowoodo blocking the path on Route 36. Also waters berries.
entries.effectText:
Required to water berries within the Berry Pots.

Required to battle the Sudowoodo on Route 36.

This item cannot be directly used from the bag.
entries.flavorText: A bottle used for watering plants in the Berry Pots.

entries.name: Squirtle Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: S.S. Ticket
entries.shortEffectText: Ticket for a ship. (RSE: S.S. Tidal LF: S.S. Anne HG: S.S. Aqua)
entries.effectText:
Allows passage on a ferry.

The same item is used for different ferries between different games.
entries.flavorText:
A ticket required for sailing on the ferry S.S. Aqua.
It has a drawing of a ship on its front.

entries.name: S.S. Ticket
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for ss-ticket--hoenn
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for ss-ticket--hoenn
entries.flavorText: Ticket required for sailing on a ferry.

entries.name: S.S. Ticket
entries.flavorText:
The ticket required to board the S.S. Anne.
It has a drawing of a ship on it.

entries.name: Stable Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Berries stay on the plant for longer than their usual time.
entries.effectText:
Used on a patch of soil
:   Fully-grown plant will last 25% longer before dying and possibly regrowing.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Star Piece
entries.shortEffectText: Platinum: Trade for one of each color Shard. Black and White: Trade for PP Up. Sell for 4900 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 9800 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A small shard of a beautiful gem that gives off
a distinctly red sparkle. It can be sold at a high price
to shops.

entries.name: Star Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A star-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it spins around happily.

entries.name: Stardust
entries.shortEffectText: Sell for 1000 Pokédollars, or to Ore Collector for 2000 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
Lovely red sand that flows between the fingers
with a loose, silky feel. It can be sold at a low price
to shops.

entries.name: Starf Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/4 max HP to boost a random stat by two stages.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/4 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to raise a random stat by two stages.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. If a Pokémon
holds one, one of its stats will sharply increase
when it’s in a pinch.

entries.name: Staryu Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Steel Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Steel-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Steel-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of steel. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Steel-type move
one time.

entries.name: Steel Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of cool
mechanical designs.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Steel Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Steel form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Steel.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Steel form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Steel.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Steel-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Steelium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for steelium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for steelium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Steel-type moves to Steel-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Steelium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Steel moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Steel moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Steel-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Steelixite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Steelix to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Steelix to Mega Evolve into Mega Steelix.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Steelix hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Leek
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Farfetch’d’s critical hit ratio by two stages.
entries.effectText: Held by Farfetch’d: Raises the holder’s critical hit counter by 2.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Farfetch’d. This very long
and stiff stalk of leek boosts its critical-hit ratio.

entries.name: Sticky Barb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Holder takes 1/8 (12.5%) its max HP at the end of each turn. When the holder is hit by a contact move, the attacking Pokémon takes 1/8 its max HP in damage and receive the item if not holding one.
entries.effectText: Held: Damaged the holder for 1/8 its max HP.  When the holder is struck by a contact move, damages the attacker for 1/8 its max HP; if the attacker is not holding an item, it will take this item.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It damages the
holder every turn and may latch on to Pokémon
that touch the holder.

entries.name: Stone Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Rock-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Rock.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Rock moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Rock.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

entries.name: Storage Key
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to the Team Galactic warehouse in Veilstone City.
entries.effectText: Grants access to the Team Galactic warehouse in Veilstone City.
entries.flavorText:
The key to Team Galactic’s sinister warehouse
located at the edge of Veilstone City.

entries.name: Storage Key
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for storage-key--galactic-warehouse
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for storage-key--galactic-warehouse
entries.flavorText:
The key to Team Galactic’s sinister warehouse
located at the edge of Veilstone City.

entries.name: Storage Key
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for storage-key--sea-mauville
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for storage-key--sea-mauville
entries.flavorText:
A key that opens the storage hold inside
Sea Mauville.

entries.name: Strange Souvenir
entries.shortEffectText: Vendor trash.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
An ornament depicting a mysterious Pokémon
that has been venerated as a guardian deity
for an extremely long time in the Alola region.

entries.name: Strawberry Sweet
entries.flavorText:
A strawberry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery
holds this, it will spin around happily.

entries.name: Stretchy Spring
entries.flavorText:
A thin small spring. It’s totally stretched out, and
what it’s used for is unknown.

names.ja-hrkt: たてストライプカップ
names.ja: たてストライプカップ

entries.name: Style Card
entries.flavorText:
A card that increases the selection in boutiques and
hair salons.

entries.name: Suite Key
entries.shortEffectText: Opens a locked building in the Lakeside Resort.
entries.effectText: Opens the locked building in the lakeside resort.
entries.flavorText:
A key to one of the suites at a certain luxury hotel
by a lake. For some odd reason, it often disappears.

entries.name: Sun Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves Nebby into Solgaleo when used at the Altar of the Sunne.
entries.effectText: Evolves Nebby into Solgaleo when used at the Altar of the Sunne.
entries.flavorText:
It is said that the tones it produces were offered
up as an expression of gratitude to the Legendary
Pokémon of the sun.

entries.name: Sun Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves a Cottonee into Whimsicott, a Gloom into Bellossom, a Petilil into Lilligant, or a Sunkern into Sunflora.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves a Cottonee into Whimsicott, a Gloom into Bellossom, a Petilil into Lilligant, or a Sunkern into Sunflora.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It burns as red as the evening sun.

names.ja-hrkt: あさやけおはなピック
names.ja: あさやけおはなピック

names.ja-hrkt: ゆうやけおはなピック
names.ja: ゆうやけおはなピック

entries.name: Super Lure
entries.flavorText:
A glass bottle of perfume that makes rare Pokémon
more likely to appear. It lasts longer than a Lure.

entries.name: Super Potion
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 50 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Restores 50 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A spray-type medicine for treating wounds. It can
be used to restore 60 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Super Repel
entries.shortEffectText: For 200 steps, prevents wild encounters of level lower than your party’s lead Pokémon.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Trainer will skip encounters with wild Pokémon of a lower level than the lead party Pokémon.  This effect wears off after the trainer takes 200 steps.
entries.flavorText:
An item that prevents any low-level wild Pokémon
from jumping out at you for a while. It lasts longer
than Repel.

entries.name: Super Rod
entries.shortEffectText: Used to catch Pokémon in bodies of water.
entries.effectText: Used to find Pokémon on the Super Rod list for an area, which are generally the best available there.
entries.flavorText:
An awesome, high-tech fishing rod. Use it at any
body of water to fish for wild aquatic Pokémon.

entries.name: Surge Badge
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for surge-badge
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for surge-badge
entries.flavorText:
A novelty badge that you can receive at the
Kantonian Gym. Rumor has it that it imitates
the Badge of a Gym somewhere.

entries.name: Surprise Mulch
entries.shortEffectText: Increases the chance of Berry mutation.
entries.effectText: Increases the chance of Berry mutation.
entries.flavorText:
A fertilizer for growing Berries. However, it appears
to be unsuitable for the soil in the Hoenn region and
has no effect.

entries.name: Swampertite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Swampert to Mega Evolve into Mega Swampert.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Swampert to Mega Evolve into Mega Swampert.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Swampert hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Sweet Apple
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally sweet.

entries.name: Sweet Heart
entries.shortEffectText: Restores 20 HP.
entries.effectText:
Used on a friendly Pokémon
:   Restores 20 HP.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of cloyingly sweet chocolate.
It can be used to restore 20 HP to a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Swift Feather
entries.shortEffectText: Increases Speed effort by 1.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases the target’s Speed effort by 1.
entries.flavorText:
An item for use on a Pokémon. It slightly increases
the Speed base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Tamato Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Drops Speed Effort Values by 10 and raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases happiness by 10/5/2.  Lowers Speed effort by 10.
entries.flavorText:
A Berry to be consumed by Pokémon. Using it on a
Pokémon makes it more friendly but lowers its
Speed base points.

entries.name: Tanga Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Bug-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Bug-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Bug-type attack.

entries.name: Tangela Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Tapunium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for tapunium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for tapunium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade the
tapu’s Nature’s Madness to an exclusive Z-Move.

entries.name: Tapunium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows the Tapus to upgrade Nature’s Madness into Guardian of Alola.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Tapu Koko, Tapu Lele, Tapu Bulu, and Tapu Fini to upgrade Nature’s Madness into Guardian of Alola.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power. It upgrades
the tapu’s Nature’s Madness to a Z-Move.

entries.name: Tart Apple
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally tart.

entries.name: Tauros Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Tea
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to Saffron City.
entries.effectText: Used to bribe the Saffron City guards for entry to the city.
entries.flavorText:
Aromatic tea that has a slightly bitter taste.
It soothes a dry throat.

entries.name: Teachy TV
entries.shortEffectText: Teachers basic trainer advice.
entries.effectText: Teaches beginning trainers basic information.
entries.flavorText:
A television set that is tuned to
a program with useful tips for
novice TRAINERS.

names.ja-hrkt: おしゃれカードみどり
names.ja: おしゃれカードみどり

entries.name: Tentacool Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Terrain Extender
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Extends the holder’s Terrain effects to 8 turns.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder changes the Terrain (whether by move or ability), it will last  8 turns instead of 5.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It extends the
duration of the terrain caused by the holder’s move
or Ability.

entries.name: Thanks Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery designed to make it easy for you to
express thanks. Have a Pokémon hold it
for delivery.

entries.name: Thick Club
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles Cubone or Marowak’s Attack.
entries.effectText: Held by Cubone or Marowak: Doubles the holder’s Attack.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by Cubone or Marowak. It’s a
hard bone of some sort that boosts the
Attack stat.

entries.name: Throat Spray
entries.flavorText:
Raises Sp. Atk when a Pokémon uses a sound-based
move.

entries.name: Thunder Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves an Eelektrik into Eelektross, an Eevee into Jolteon, or a Pikachu into Raichu.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves an Eelektrik into Eelektross, an Eevee into Jolteon, or a Pikachu into Raichu.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It has a distinct thunderbolt pattern.

entries.name: Tidal Bell
entries.shortEffectText: Allows Kimono-girls to summon Lugia.
entries.effectText: May be given to the Kimono Girls to summon Lugia to the top of the Bell Tower.
entries.flavorText:
A very old-fashioned bell that makes a gentle
ringing sound.

entries.name: Timer Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon. Success rate increases by 0.1× (Gen V: 0.3×) every turn, to a max of 4×.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon.  Has a catch rate of 1.1× on the first turn of the battle and increases by 0.1× every turn, to a maximum of 4× on turn 30.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
A somewhat different Poké Ball that becomes
progressively more effective at catching Pokémon
the more turns that are taken in battle.

entries.name: Timid Mint
entries.flavorText:
When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Speed will grow
more easily, but its Attack will grow more slowly.

entries.name: Tin of Beans
entries.flavorText:
One of the many ingredients that can be used for
cooking at your camp. When simmered, the beans
suffuse a dish with their mild flavor.

entries.name: Tiny Mushroom
entries.shortEffectText: Fire Red and Leaf Green: Trade two for prior Level-up moves. Sell for 250 Pokédollars, or to Hungry Maid for 500 Pokédollars.
entries.effectText: Vendor trash.
entries.flavorText:
A very small and rare mushroom. It’s popular with
a certain class of collectors.

entries.name: TM Case
entries.shortEffectText: Holds TMs.
entries.effectText: Stores TMs and HMs.

entries.name: TM00
entries.flavorText:
The target is slugged by a punch thrown with
muscle-packed power.

entries.name: TM01
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Hone Claws to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Focus Punch Gen II: DynamicPunch Gen I: Mega Punch)
entries.effectText: Teaches Focus Punch to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is attacked by a kick launched with
muscle-packed power.

entries.name: TM02
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Dragon Claw to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Headbutt Gen I: Razor Wind)
entries.effectText: Teaches Dragon Claw to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Numerous coins are hurled at the target to inflict
damage. Money is earned after the battle.

entries.name: TM03
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Psyshock to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Water Pulse Gen II: Curse Gen I: Swords Dance)
entries.effectText: Teaches Water Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is punched with a fiery fist. This may also
leave the target with a burn.

entries.name: TM04
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Calm Mind to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Rollout Gen I: Whirlwind)
entries.effectText: Teaches Calm Mind to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is punched with an icy fist. This may also
leave the target frozen.

entries.name: TM05
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Roar to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Mega Kick)
entries.effectText: Teaches Roar to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is punched with an electrified fist.
This may also leave the target with paralysis.

entries.name: TM06
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Toxic to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Toxic to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user flies up into the sky and then strikes its target
on the next turn.

entries.name: TM07
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Hail to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Zap Cannon Gen I: Horn Drill)
entries.effectText: Teaches Hail to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Sharp spikes are shot at the target in rapid
succession. They hit two to five times in a row.

entries.name: TM08
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Bulk Up to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Rock Smash Gen I: Body Slam)
entries.effectText: Teaches Bulk Up to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is attacked with a powerful beam.
The user can’t move on the next turn.

entries.name: TM09
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Venoshock to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Bullet Seed Gen II: Psych Up Gen I: Take Down)
entries.effectText: Teaches Bullet Seed to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user charges at the target using every bit of its
power. The user can’t move on the next turn.

entries.name: TM10
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Hidden Power to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Double-Edge)
entries.effectText: Teaches Hidden Power to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user scatters curious leaves that chase the
target. This attack never misses.

entries.name: TM100
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM100.
entries.effectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM100.
entries.flavorText:
The user tells the target a secret, and the target
loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers
the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TM11
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Sunny Day to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: BubbleBeam)
entries.effectText: Teaches Sunny Day to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
In this two-turn attack, the user gathers light, then
blasts a bundled beam on the next turn.

entries.name: TM12
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Taunt to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Sweet Scent Gen I: Water Gun)
entries.effectText: Teaches Taunt to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
In this two-turn attack, the user gathers light and fills a
blade with the light’s energy, attacking the target on the
next turn.

entries.name: TM13
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Ice Beam to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Snore)
entries.effectText: Teaches Ice Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target becomes trapped within a fierce vortex
of fire that rages for four to five turns.

entries.name: TM14
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Blizzard to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Blizzard to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user launches a weak jolt of electricity that
paralyzes the target.

entries.name: TM15
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Hyper Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Hyper Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user burrows into the ground, then attacks on
the next turn.

entries.name: TM16
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Light Screen to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Icy Wind Gen I: Pay Day)
entries.effectText: Teaches Light Screen to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
An earsplitting screech harshly lowers the target’s
Defense stat.

entries.name: TM17
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Protect to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Submission)
entries.effectText: Teaches Protect to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage
from special attacks for five turns.

entries.name: TM18
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rain Dance to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Counter)
entries.effectText: Teaches Rain Dance to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage
from physical attacks for five turns.

entries.name: TM19
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Telekinesis to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Giga Drain Gen I: Seismic Toss)
entries.effectText: Teaches Giga Drain to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user creates a protective field that prevents
status conditions for five turns.

entries.name: TM20
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Safeguard to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Endure Gen I: Rage)
entries.effectText: Teaches Safeguard to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks everything around it by causing an
explosion. The user faints upon using this move.

entries.name: TM21
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Frustration to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Mega Drain)
entries.effectText: Teaches Frustration to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user goes to sleep for two turns. This fully
restores the user’s HP and heals any
status conditions.

entries.name: TM22
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches SolarBeam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches SolarBeam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Large boulders are hurled at opposing Pokémon to
inflict damage. This may also make the opposing
Pokémon flinch.

entries.name: TM23
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Smack Down to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Iron Tail Gen I: Dragon Rage)
entries.effectText: Teaches Iron Tail to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks and steals the target’s held item
simultaneously. The user can’t steal anything if it
already holds an item.

entries.name: TM24
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Thunderbolt to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: DragonBreath)
entries.effectText: Teaches Thunderbolt to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This attack can be used only if the user is asleep.
The harsh noise may also make the target flinch.

entries.name: TM25
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Thunder to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Thunder to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This move enables the user to protect itself from
all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used
in succession.

entries.name: TM26
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Earthquake to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Earthquake to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user frightens the target with a scary face to
harshly lower its Speed stat.

entries.name: TM27
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Return to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Fissure)
entries.effectText: Teaches Return to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also
lowers opposing Pokémon’s Speed stats.

entries.name: TM28
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Dig to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Dig to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A nutrient-draining attack. The user’s HP is restored
by half the damage taken by the target.

entries.name: TM29
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Psychic to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Psychic to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user gazes at the target rather charmingly,
making it less wary. This harshly lowers the target’s
Attack stat.

entries.name: TM30
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Shadow Ball to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Teleport)
entries.effectText: Teaches Shadow Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The target is hit with wings of steel. This may also
raise the user’s Defense stat.

entries.name: TM31
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Brick Break to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Mud-Slap Gen I: Mimic)
entries.effectText: Teaches Brick Break to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
If it is the opposite gender of the user, the target
becomes infatuated and less likely to attack.

entries.name: TM32
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Double Team to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Double Team to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A five-turn sandstorm is summoned to hurt all
combatants except Rock, Ground, and Steel types.
It raises the Sp. Def stat of Rock types.

entries.name: TM33
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Reflect to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Ice Punch)
entries.effectText: Teaches Reflect to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user summons a heavy rain that falls for five
turns, powering up Water-type moves. It lowers
the power of Fire-type moves.

entries.name: TM34
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Sludge Wave to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Shock Wave Gen II: Swagger Gen I: Bide)
entries.effectText: Teaches Shock Wave to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user intensifies the sun for five turns, powering
up Fire-type moves. It lowers the power of
Water-type moves.

entries.name: TM35
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Flamethrower to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Sleep Talk Gen I: Metronome)
entries.effectText: Teaches Flamethrower to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user summons a hailstorm lasting five turns.
It damages all Pokémon except Ice types.

entries.name: TM36
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Sludge Bomb to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Selfdestruct)
entries.effectText: Teaches Sludge Bomb to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user traps the target in a violent swirling
whirlpool for four to five turns.

entries.name: TM37
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Sandstorm to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Egg Bomb)
entries.effectText: Teaches Sandstorm to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user gets all party Pokémon to attack the target.
The more party Pokémon, the greater the number
of attacks.

entries.name: TM38
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Fire Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Fire Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user shoots a sinister flame at the target to inflict
a burn.

entries.name: TM39
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rock Tomb to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II & I: Swift)
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Tomb to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This attack move doubles its power if the user is
poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

entries.name: TM40
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Aerial Ace to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Defense Curl Gen I: Skull Bash)
entries.effectText: Teaches Aerial Ace to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Star-shaped rays are shot at opposing Pokémon.
This attack never misses.

entries.name: TM41
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Torment to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: ThunderPunch Gen I: Softboiled)
entries.effectText: Teaches Torment to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user assists an ally by boosting the power of
that ally’s attack.

entries.name: TM42
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Facade to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II & I: Dream Eater)
entries.effectText: Teaches Facade to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This attack move’s power is doubled if the user has
been hurt by the opponent in the same turn.

entries.name: TM43
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Flame Charge to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Secret Power Gen II: Detect Gen I: Sky Attack)
entries.effectText: Teaches Secret Power to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks with a swift chop. It can also break
barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

entries.name: TM44
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rest to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Rest to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by
the user, they are prevented from using it.

entries.name: TM45
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Attract to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Thunder Wave)
entries.effectText: Teaches Attract to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
