Diving on the first turn, the user floats up and
attacks on the next turn.

entries.name: TM46
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Thief to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen I: Psywave)
entries.effectText: Teaches Thief to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This attack move varies in power and type
depending on the weather.

entries.name: TM47
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Low Sweep to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III & II: Steel Wing Gen I: Explosion)
entries.effectText: Teaches Steel Wing to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user feigns crying to fluster the target, harshly
lowering its Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TM48
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Round to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Skill Swap Gen II: Fire Punch Gen I: Rock Slide)
entries.effectText: Teaches Skill Swap to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
Boulders are hurled at the target. This also lowers
the target’s Speed stat by preventing its movement.

entries.name: TM49
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Echoed Voice to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV & III: Snatch Gen II: Fury Cutter Gen I: Tri Attack)
entries.effectText: Teaches Snatch to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user traps the target inside a harshly raging
sandstorm for four to five turns.

entries.name: TM50
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Overheat to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen II: Nightmare Gen I: Substitute)
entries.effectText: Teaches Overheat to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user forcefully shoots seeds at the target two
to five times in a row.

entries.name: TM51
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Ally Switch to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Roost)
entries.effectText: Teaches Roost to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user launches sharp icicles at the target two to
five times in a row.

entries.name: TM52
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Focus Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Focus Blast to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user bounces up high, then drops on the target
on the second turn. This may also leave the target
with paralysis.

entries.name: TM53
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Energy Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Energy Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by hurling a blob of mud at the
target. This also lowers the target’s Speed stat.

entries.name: TM54
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches False Swipe to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches False Swipe to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user hurls hard rocks at the target. Two to five
rocks are launched in a row.

entries.name: TM55
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Scald to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Brine)
entries.effectText: Teaches Brine to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
If the target’s HP is half or less, this attack will hit
with double the power.

entries.name: TM56
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Fling to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Fling to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
After making its attack, the user rushes back to
switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

entries.name: TM57
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Charge Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Charge Beam to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user stores power, then attacks. If the user
moves after the target, this attack’s power will
be doubled.

entries.name: TM58
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Sky Drop to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Endure)
entries.effectText: Teaches Endure to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
If the target has already taken some damage in the
same turn, this attack’s power is doubled.

entries.name: TM59
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Incinerate to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Dragon Pulse)
entries.effectText: Teaches Dragon Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user flings its held item at the target to attack.
This move’s power and effects depend on the item.

entries.name: TM60
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Quash to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Drain Punch)
entries.effectText: Teaches Drain Punch to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user employs its psychic power to switch
changes to its Attack and Sp. Atk stats with
the target.

entries.name: TM61
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Will-O-Wisp to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Will-O-Wisp to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user employs its psychic power to switch
changes to its Defense and Sp. Def stats with
the target.

entries.name: TM62
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Acrobatics to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Silver Wind)
entries.effectText: Teaches Silver Wind to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText: The user exchanges Speed stats with the target.

entries.name: TM63
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Embargo to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Embargo to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
An energy-draining punch. The user’s HP is restored
by half the damage taken by the target.

entries.name: TM64
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Explosion to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Explosion to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The power of this attack move is doubled if the user
has been hurt by the target in the same turn.

entries.name: TM65
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Shadow Claw to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Shadow Claw to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user slashes with a sharp claw made from
shadows. Critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TM66
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Payback to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Payback to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user bites with electrified fangs. This may also
make the target flinch or leave it with paralysis.

entries.name: TM67
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Retaliate to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Recycle)
entries.effectText: Teaches Recycle to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user bites with cold-infused fangs. This may
also make the target flinch or leave it frozen.

entries.name: TM68
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Giga Impact to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Giga Impact to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user bites with flame-cloaked fangs. This may
also make the target flinch or leave it with a burn.

entries.name: TM69
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rock Polish to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Polish to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user tears at the target with blades formed by
psychic power. Critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TM70
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Flash to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Flash to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user creates a bizarre area in which slower
Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

entries.name: TM71
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Stone Edge to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Stone Edge to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s
Defense and Sp. Def stats are swapped for
five turns.

entries.name: TM72
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Volt Switch to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Avalanche)
entries.effectText: Teaches Avalanche to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s
held items lose their effects for five turns.

entries.name: TM73
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Thunder Wave to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Thunder Wave to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A slashing attack with a poisonous blade that may
also poison the target. Critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TM74
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Gyro Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Gyro Ball to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user drenches the target in a special poisonous
liquid. This move’s power is doubled if the target
is poisoned.

entries.name: TM75
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Swords Dance to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Swords Dance to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user makes a swift attack on the target’s legs,
which lowers the target’s Speed stat.

entries.name: TM76
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Struggle Bug to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Stealth Rock)
entries.effectText: Teaches Stealth Rock to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the target with a song. Others can
join in the Round to increase the power of
the attack.

entries.name: TM77
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Psych Up to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Psych Up to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This relentless attack does massive damage to a
target affected by status conditions.

entries.name: TM78
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Bulldoze to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Captivate)
entries.effectText: Teaches Captivate to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user nimbly strikes the target. If the user is not
holding an item, this attack inflicts massive damage.

entries.name: TM79
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Frost Breath to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Dark Pulse)
entries.effectText: Teaches Dark Pulse to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user gets revenge for a fainted ally. If an ally
fainted in the previous turn, this move’s power
is increased.

entries.name: TM80
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rock Slide to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Slide to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
After making its attack, the user rushes back to
switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

entries.name: TM81
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches X-Scissor to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches X-Scissor to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user strikes everything around it by stomping
down on the ground. This lowers the Speed stats of
those hit.

entries.name: TM82
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Dragon Tail to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Sleep Talk)
entries.effectText: Teaches Sleep Talk to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks and captures opposing Pokémon
using an electric net. This lowers their Speed stats.

entries.name: TM83
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Work Up to a compatible Pokémon. (Gen IV: Natural Gift)
entries.effectText: Teaches Natural Gift to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user cuts its target with sharp shells. This may
also lower the target’s Defense stat.

entries.name: TM84
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Poison Jab to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Poison Jab to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by striking the target with its hard
tail. It hits the target two to five times in a row.

entries.name: TM85
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Dream Eater to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Dream Eater to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user yells as if it’s ranting about something,
which lowers the Sp. Atk stats of opposing Pokémon.

entries.name: TM86
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Grass Knot to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Grass Knot to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user vanishes somewhere, then strikes the
target on the next turn. This move hits even if the
target protects itself.

entries.name: TM87
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Swagger to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Swagger to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user steals the target’s HP with a kiss. The
user’s HP is restored by over half of the damage
taken by the target.

entries.name: TM88
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Pluck to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Pluck to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user turns the ground to grass for five turns.
This restores the HP of Pokémon on the ground a
little every turn and powers up Grass-type moves.

entries.name: TM89
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches U-turn to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches U-Turn to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This protects Pokémon on the ground from status
conditions and halves damage from Dragon-type
moves for five turns.

entries.name: TM90
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Substitute to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Substitute to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user electrifies the ground for five turns,
powering up Electric-type moves. Pokémon on the
ground no longer fall asleep.

entries.name: TM91
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Flash Cannon to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Flash Cannon to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
This protects Pokémon on the ground from priority
moves and powers up Psychic-type moves for
five turns.

entries.name: TM92
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Trick Room to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Trick Room to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by breathing a special, hot fire.
This also lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TM93
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Wild Charge to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Wild Charge to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user’s body generates an eerie impulse.
Exposing the target to it harshly lowers the target’s
Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TM94
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Rock Smash to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Rock Smash to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
A restrained attack that prevents the target from
fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.

entries.name: TM95
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches Snarl to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.effectText: Teaches Snarl to a compatible Pokémon.
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks with a blade of air that slices even
the sky. This may also make the target flinch.

entries.name: TM96
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM96.
entries.effectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM96.
entries.flavorText:
The user stabs the target with a sharp horn.
This attack never misses.

entries.name: TM97
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM97.
entries.effectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM97.
entries.flavorText:
The user swings its body around violently to inflict
damage on everything in its vicinity.

entries.name: TM98
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM98.
entries.effectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM98.
entries.flavorText:
Driven by frustration, the user attacks the target.
If the user’s previous move has failed, the power
of this move doubles.

entries.name: TM99
entries.shortEffectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM99.
entries.effectText: Teaches a Pokémon TM99.
entries.flavorText:
The user swings its tough tail wildly and attacks
opposing Pokémon. This also lowers their Attack stats.

entries.name: TMV Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Permits access to Kiloude City.
entries.effectText: Permits access to Kiloude City.
entries.flavorText:
A commuter pass that allows the holder to ride the
TMV between Lumiose City and Kiloude City at
any time.

names.ja-hrkt: ノノクラゲのヒラヒラ
names.ja: ノノクラゲのヒラヒラ

entries.name: Tough Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon, it will increase that Pokémon’s Defense stat.

entries.name: Tough Candy L
entries.flavorText:
A big candy that is packed with energy. When given to
a Pokémon at Lv. 30 or higher, it will increase
that Pokémon’s Defense stat.

entries.name: Tough Candy XL
entries.flavorText:
A huge candy that is packed with energy. When given
to a Pokémon at Lv. 60 or higher, it will increase
that Pokémon’s Defense stat.

entries.name: Town Map
entries.shortEffectText: Use to see the overworld map.
entries.effectText: Displays a map of the region including the trainer’s position, location names, visited towns, gym locations, and where the trainer has been walking recently.
entries.flavorText:
A very convenient map that can be viewed anytime.
It even shows you your present location in the region.

entries.name: Toxic Orb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Inflicts Toxic on the holder at the end of the turn. Activates after Poison damage would occur.
entries.effectText: Held: Badly poisons the holder at the end of each turn.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a bizarre orb
that will badly poison the holder during battle.

entries.name: Toxic Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Poison-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Poison.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Poison moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Poison.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Poison-type moves.

entries.name: TR00
entries.flavorText:
A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit.
This sharply raises the user’s Attack stat.

entries.name: TR01
entries.flavorText:
The user drops onto the target with its full body
weight. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

entries.name: TR02
entries.flavorText:
The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire.
This may also leave the target with a burn.

entries.name: TR03
entries.flavorText:
The target is blasted by a huge volume of water
launched under great pressure.

entries.name: TR04
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks everything around it by swamping
its surroundings with a giant wave.

entries.name: TR05
entries.flavorText:
The target is struck with an icy-cold beam of energy.
This may also leave the target frozen.

entries.name: TR06
entries.flavorText:
A howling blizzard is summoned to strike opposing
Pokémon. This may also leave the opposing
Pokémon frozen.

entries.name: TR07
entries.flavorText:
A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over.
The heavier the target, the greater the move’s power.

entries.name: TR08
entries.flavorText:
A strong electric blast crashes down on the target.
This may also leave the target with paralysis.

entries.name: TR09
entries.flavorText:
A wicked thunderbolt is dropped on the target to
inflict damage. This may also leave the target
with paralysis.

entries.name: TR10
entries.flavorText:
The user sets off an earthquake that strikes every
Pokémon around it.

entries.name: TR11
entries.flavorText:
The target is hit by a strong telekinetic force.
This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR12
entries.flavorText:
The user relaxes and lightens its body to move
faster. This sharply raises the Speed stat.

entries.name: TR13
entries.flavorText:
The user takes a deep breath and focuses so that
critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TR14
entries.flavorText:
The user waggles a finger and stimulates its brain
into randomly using nearly any move.

entries.name: TR15
entries.flavorText:
The target is attacked with an intense blast of
all-consuming fire. This may also leave the target
with a burn.

entries.name: TR16
entries.flavorText:
The user charges at the target and may make
it flinch.

entries.name: TR17
entries.flavorText:
The user temporarily empties its mind to forget its
concerns. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR18
entries.flavorText:
The user drains the target’s blood. The user’s HP is
restored by half the damage taken by the target.

entries.name: TR19
entries.flavorText:
The user strikes with a simultaneous three-beam attack.
This may also burn, freeze, or paralyze the target.

entries.name: TR20
entries.flavorText:
The user creates a substitute for itself using some of
its HP. The substitute serves as the user’s decoy.

entries.name: TR21
entries.flavorText:
An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the
less HP the user has.

entries.name: TR22
entries.flavorText:
Unsanitary sludge is hurled at the target. This may
also poison the target.

entries.name: TR23
entries.flavorText:
The user lays a trap of spikes at the opposing
team’s feet. The trap hurts Pokémon that switch
into battle.

entries.name: TR24
entries.flavorText:
The user rampages and attacks for two to three
turns. The user then becomes confused.

entries.name: TR25
entries.flavorText:
The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack
the target. This attack does physical damage.

entries.name: TR26
entries.flavorText:
The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP.
Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

entries.name: TR27
entries.flavorText:
While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the
moves it knows.

entries.name: TR28
entries.flavorText:
Using its tough and impressive horn, the user rams
into the target with no letup.

entries.name: TR29
entries.flavorText:
The user switches places with a party Pokémon in
waiting and passes along any stat changes.

entries.name: TR30
entries.flavorText:
The user compels the target to keep using the move
it encored for three turns.

entries.name: TR31
entries.flavorText:
The target is slammed with a steel-hard tail.
This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

entries.name: TR32
entries.flavorText:
The user crunches up the target with sharp fangs.
This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

entries.name: TR33
entries.flavorText:
The user hurls a shadowy blob at the target.
This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR34
entries.flavorText:
Two turns after this move is used, a hunk of psychic
energy attacks the target.

entries.name: TR35
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks in an uproar for three turns.
During that time, no Pokémon can fall asleep.

entries.name: TR36
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by exhaling hot breath on opposing
Pokémon. This may also leave those Pokémon with
a burn.

entries.name: TR37
entries.flavorText:
The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use
only attack moves for three turns.

entries.name: TR38
entries.flavorText:
The user catches the target off guard and swaps its
held item with its own.

entries.name: TR39
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the target with great power.
However, this also lowers the user’s Attack and
Defense stats.

entries.name: TR40
entries.flavorText:
The user employs its psychic power to exchange
Abilities with the target.

entries.name: TR41
entries.flavorText:
The user launches a kick that lands a critical hit
more easily. This may also leave the target with
a burn.

entries.name: TR42
entries.flavorText:
The user lets loose a horribly echoing shout with
the power to inflict damage.

entries.name: TR43
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the target at full power. The attack’s
recoil harshly lowers the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TR44
entries.flavorText:
The user absorbs a mystical power from space to
raise its Defense and Sp. Def stats.

entries.name: TR45
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by shooting muddy water at opposing
Pokémon. This may also lower their accuracy.

entries.name: TR46
entries.flavorText:
The user hardens its body’s surface like iron,
sharply raising its Defense stat.

entries.name: TR47
entries.flavorText: The user slashes the target with huge sharp claws.

entries.name: TR48
entries.flavorText:
The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body,
raising both its Attack and Defense stats.

entries.name: TR49
entries.flavorText:
The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its
spirit to raise its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

entries.name: TR50
entries.flavorText:
The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and
attacks by cutting its target. Critical hits land
more easily.

entries.name: TR51
entries.flavorText:
The user vigorously performs a mystic, powerful
dance that raises its Attack and Speed stats.

entries.name: TR52
entries.flavorText:
The user tackles the target with a high-speed spin.
The slower the user compared to the target, the
greater the move’s power.

entries.name: TR53
entries.flavorText:
The user fights the target up close without guarding
itself. This also lowers the user’s Defense and
Sp. Def stats.

entries.name: TR54
entries.flavorText:
The user lays a trap of poison spikes at the feet of
the opposing team. The spikes will poison opposing
Pokémon that switch into battle.

entries.name: TR55
entries.flavorText:
The user cloaks itself in fire and charges the target.
This also damages the user quite a lot. This attack
may leave the target with a burn.

entries.name: TR56
entries.flavorText:
The user lets loose a blast of aura power from deep
within its body at the target. This attack
never misses.

entries.name: TR57
entries.flavorText:
The target is stabbed with a tentacle, arm, or the like
steeped in poison. This may also poison the target.

entries.name: TR58
entries.flavorText:
The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark
thoughts. This may also make the target flinch.

entries.name: TR59
entries.flavorText:
The user slams a barrage of hard-shelled seeds
down on the target from above.

entries.name: TR60
entries.flavorText:
The user slashes at the target by crossing its
scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.

entries.name: TR61
entries.flavorText:
The user generates a damaging sound wave by
vibration. This may also lower the target’s
Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR62
entries.flavorText:
The target is attacked with a shock wave generated
by the user’s gaping mouth.

entries.name: TR63
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks with a ray of light that sparkles as
if it were made of gemstones.

entries.name: TR64
entries.flavorText:
The user heightens its mental focus and unleashes
its power. This may also lower the target’s
Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR65
entries.flavorText:
The user draws power from nature and fires it at the
target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR66
entries.flavorText:
The user tucks in its wings and charges from a low
altitude. This also damages the user quite a lot.

entries.name: TR67
entries.flavorText:
The user makes the ground under the target erupt
with power. This may also lower the target’s
Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR68
entries.flavorText:
The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad
thoughts. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TR69
entries.flavorText:
The user focuses its willpower to its head and
attacks the target. This may also make the
target flinch.

entries.name: TR70
entries.flavorText:
The user gathers all its light energy and releases it
all at once. This may also lower the target’s
Sp. Def stat.

entries.name: TR71
entries.flavorText:
The user whips up a storm of leaves around the
target. The attack’s recoil harshly lowers the user’s
Sp. Atk stat.

entries.name: TR72
entries.flavorText:
The user violently whirls its vines, tentacles, or the
like to harshly lash the target.

entries.name: TR73
entries.flavorText:
The user shoots filthy garbage at the target to
attack. This may also poison the target.

entries.name: TR74
entries.flavorText:
The user slams the target with its steel-hard head.
This may also make the target flinch.

entries.name: TR75
entries.flavorText:
The user stabs the target from below with
sharpened stones. Critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TR76
entries.flavorText:
The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the
opposing team. The trap hurts opposing Pokémon
that switch into battle.

entries.name: TR77
entries.flavorText:
The user snares the target with grass and trips it.
The heavier the target, the greater the
move’s power.

entries.name: TR78
entries.flavorText:
The user strikes everything around it by swamping
the area with a giant sludge wave. This may also
poison those hit.

entries.name: TR79
entries.flavorText:
The user slams into the target with its heavy body.
The more the user outweighs the target, the greater
the move’s power.

entries.name: TR80
entries.flavorText:
The user hurls an electric orb at the target. The
faster the user is than the target, the greater the
move’s power.

entries.name: TR81
entries.flavorText:
The user turns the target’s power against it.
The higher the target’s Attack stat, the greater
the damage it deals.

entries.name: TR82
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the target with stored power.
The more the user’s stats are raised, the greater
the move’s power.

entries.name: TR83
entries.flavorText:
The user teleports using a strange power and
switches places with one of its allies.

entries.name: TR84
entries.flavorText:
The user shoots boiling hot water at its target.
This may also leave the target with a burn.

entries.name: TR85
entries.flavorText:
The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk
stats increase.

entries.name: TR86
entries.flavorText:
The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes
into its target. This also damages the user a little.

entries.name: TR87
entries.flavorText:
The user crashes into its target while rotating its
body like a drill. Critical hits land more easily.

entries.name: TR88
entries.flavorText:
The user slams its target with its flame-covered
body. The more the user outweighs the target, the
greater the move’s power.

entries.name: TR89
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a
fierce wind that flies up into the sky. This may also
confuse the target.

entries.name: TR90
entries.flavorText:
The user plays rough with the target and attacks it.
This may also lower the target’s Attack stat.

entries.name: TR91
entries.flavorText:
Opposing Pokémon are drenched in an odd
poisonous liquid. This lowers the Attack, Sp. Atk, and
Speed stats of a poisoned target.

entries.name: TR92
entries.flavorText:
The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a
powerful flash.

entries.name: TR93
entries.flavorText:
The user swings both arms and hits the target.
The target’s stat changes don’t affect this
attack’s damage.

entries.name: TR94
entries.flavorText:
The user fiercely attacks the target using its
entire body.

entries.name: TR95
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the target’s throat, and the
resultant suffering prevents the target from using
moves that emit sound for two turns.

entries.name: TR96
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks the enemy with a pollen puff that
explodes. If the target is an ally, it gives the ally a
pollen puff that restores its HP instead.

entries.name: TR97
entries.flavorText:
The user bites the target with its psychic
capabilities. This can also destroy Light Screen
and Reflect.

entries.name: TR98
entries.flavorText:
The user slams into the target using a full-force
blast of water. This may also lower the target’s
Defense stat.

entries.name: TR99
entries.flavorText:
The user attacks by slamming its body into the target.
The higher the user’s Defense, the more damage it can
inflict on the target.

entries.name: Travel Trunk
entries.shortEffectText: Unobtainable, but allows the player to change clothes anywhere.
entries.effectText: Unobtainable, but allows the player to change clothes anywhere.
entries.flavorText:
A light and yet incredibly effective trunk, which can
contain every last piece of clothing that can
be bought.

entries.name: Tri-Pass
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to the first three Sevii Islands.
entries.effectText: Provides access to the first three Sevii Islands.
entries.flavorText:
A pass for ferries between ONE,
TWO, and THREE ISLAND.
It has a drawing of three islands.

entries.name: Tropic Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
BELLOSSOM print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Tropical Shell
entries.flavorText:
A beautiful white shell that may have drifted from
a sea in a warm region.

entries.name: Tunnel Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
Stationery featuring a print of a dimly
lit coal mine.
Let a Pokémon hold it for delivery.

entries.name: Twisted Spoon
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Psychic-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Psychic moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This spoon is
imbued with telekinetic power and boosts
Psychic-type moves.

names.ja-hrkt: シビシラスのねんえき
names.ja: シビシラスのねんえき

entries.name: Tyranitarite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Tyranitar to Mega Evolve into Mega Tyranitar.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Tyranitar to Mega Evolve into Mega Tyranitar.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Tyranitar hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Ultra Ball
entries.shortEffectText: Tries to catch a wild Pokémon.  Success rate is 2×.
entries.effectText:
Used in battle
:   Attempts to catch a wild Pokémon, using a catch rate of 2×.

    If used in a trainer battle, nothing happens and the ball is lost.
entries.flavorText:
An ultra-high-performance Poké Ball that provides
a higher success rate for catching Pokémon than
a Great Ball.

entries.name: Ultranecrozium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for ultranecrozium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for ultranecrozium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It’s a crystal that turns Necrozma fused with
Solgaleo or Lunala into a new form.

entries.name: Ultranecrozium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for ultranecrozium-z--held
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for ultranecrozium-z--held
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Necrozma’s Photon Geyser to
a Z-Move.

entries.name: Unown Report
entries.shortEffectText: Keeps track of Unown types caught.
entries.effectText: Lists which Unown forms the trainer has caught.
entries.flavorText: A report of all the discovered kinds of Unown.

entries.name: Upgrade
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Porygon: Holder evolves into Porygon2.
entries.effectText: Held by Porygon: Evolves the holder into Porygon2 when traded.
entries.flavorText:
A transparent device somehow filled with all sorts
of data. It was produced by Silph Co.

entries.name: Utility Umbrella
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This sturdy umbrella
protects the holder from the effects of rain and
harsh sunlight.

entries.name: Venonat Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Venusaurite
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows Venusaur to Mega Evolve into Mega Venusaur.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows Venusaur to Mega Evolve into Mega Venusaur.
entries.flavorText:
One of a variety of mysterious Mega Stones.
Have Venusaur hold it, and this stone will enable it
to Mega Evolve during battle.

entries.name: Voltorb Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Vs. Recorder
entries.shortEffectText: Records wireless, Wi-Fi, or Battle Frontier battles, and stores points.
entries.effectText:
Optionally records wireless, Wi-Fi, and Battle Frontier battles.

Tracks Battle Points earned in the Battle Frontier, and stores commemorative prints.
entries.flavorText:
An amazing device that can record a battle
between friends or the battles at certain special
battle facilities.

entries.name: Vs. Seeker
entries.shortEffectText: Allows rebattling of on-screen trainers. 100 steps to recharge.
entries.effectText: Reveals trainers who want a rematch, by showing !! over their heads.  Each use drains the battery; requires 100 steps to charge.
entries.flavorText:
A device that indicates Trainers who want to
battle. Its battery charges while you walk.

entries.name: Vulpix Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Wacan Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Electric-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Electric-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Electric-type attack.

entries.name: Wailmer Pail
entries.shortEffectText: Used to water berries.
entries.effectText: Waters Berry plants.
entries.flavorText:
This is a tool for watering Berries you planted to
make them grow more quickly.

entries.name: Water Gem
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder uses a damaging Water-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.effectText:
Held
:   When the holder uses a damaging Water-type move, the move has 1.5× power and this item is consumed.
entries.flavorText:
A gem with an essence of water. When held, it
strengthens the power of a Water-type move
one time.

entries.name: Water Memory
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Water form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Water.
entries.effectText: Held: Changes Silvally to its Water form.  Changes Multi-Attack’s type to Water.
entries.flavorText:
A memory disc that contains Water-type data.
It changes the type of the holder if held by
a certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Water Stone
entries.shortEffectText: Evolves an Eevee into Vaporeon, a Lombre into Ludicolo, a Panpour into Simipour, a Poliwhirl into Poliwrath, a Shellder into Cloyster, or a Staryu into Starmie.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Evolves an Eevee into Vaporeon, a Lombre into Ludicolo, a Panpour into Simipour, a Poliwhirl into Poliwrath, a Shellder into Cloyster, or a Staryu into Starmie.
entries.flavorText:
A peculiar stone that can make certain species of
Pokémon evolve. It is the blue of a pool of clear water.

entries.name: Waterium Z
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for waterium-z--bag
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for waterium-z--bag
entries.flavorText:
It converts Z-Power into crystals that upgrade
Water-type moves to Water-type Z-Moves.

entries.name: Waterium Z
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Water moves.
entries.effectText: Held: Allows a Pokémon to use the Z-move equivalents of its Water moves.
entries.flavorText:
This is a crystallized form of Z-Power.
It upgrades Water-type moves to Z-Moves.

entries.name: Watmel Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cleverness, this Berry is rare in other regions.

entries.name: Wave Incense
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Water-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Breeding: Mantine begets a Mantyke Egg.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Water moves by 20%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This incense has
a curious aroma that boosts the power of
Water-type moves.

entries.name: Wave Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
WAILMER print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Weakness Policy
entries.shortEffectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a super effective move, its Attack and Special Attack raise by two stages.
entries.effectText: Held: When the holder is hit by a super effective move, its Attack and Special Attack raise by two stages.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. Attack and
Sp. Atk sharply increase if the holder is hit with
a move it’s weak to.

entries.name: Weedle Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Wepear Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Used for creating PokéBlocks and Poffins.
entries.effectText: No effect; only useful for planting and cooking.
entries.flavorText:
Used to make Pokéblocks that will enhance your
Cleverness. Its green flesh is bitter when eaten.

entries.name: Whipped Dream
entries.shortEffectText: Traded on a Swirlix: Holder evolves into Slurpuff.
entries.effectText: Traded on a Swirlix: Holder evolves into Slurpuff.
entries.flavorText:
A soft and sweet treat made of fluffy, puffy,
whipped, and whirled cream. It’s loved by a
certain Pokémon.

entries.name: White Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Fast Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Fast Ball.
entries.flavorText: A white Apricorn. It doesn’t smell like anything.

entries.name: White Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Doubles the wild Pokémon encounter rate.
entries.effectText:
Used outside of battle
:   Doubles the wild Pokémon encounter rate.
entries.flavorText:
A flute made from white glass. It makes it easier to
encounter weak Pokémon in the place you use it.

entries.name: White Herb
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Resets all lowered stats to normal at end of turn. Consumed after use.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   At the end of each turn, if any of the holder’s stats have a negative stat modifier, the holder consumes this item to remove the modifiers from those stats.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It will restore any
lowered stat in battle. It can be used only once.

entries.name: White Mane Hair
entries.flavorText:
Mane hair that belonged to the King of Bountiful
Harvests’ beloved steed, Glastrier. The hair is strong
and very flexible.

entries.name: Wide Lens
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Provides a 1/10 (10%) boost in accuracy to the holder.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the accuracy of any move the holder uses by 10% (multiplied; i.e. 70% accuracy is increased to 77%).
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a magnifying
lens that slightly boosts the accuracy of moves.

entries.name: Wiki Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed at 1/2 max HP to restore 1/8 max HP. Confuses Pokémon that dislike dry flavor.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder has 1/2 its max HP remaining or less, it consumes this item to restore 1/8 its max HP.  If the holder dislikes dry flavors (i.e., has a nature that lowers Special Attack), it will also become confused.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, it restores the user’s HP in a
pinch, but it will cause confusion if the user hates
the taste.

entries.name: Wise Glasses
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Boosts the damage of special moves used by the holder by 1/10 (10%).
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the power of the holder’s special moves by 10%.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. This thick pair of
glasses slightly boosts the power of special moves.

entries.name: Wishing Chip
entries.flavorText:
Toss a number of them into a Pokémon Den for the
chance to attract a wild Dynamax Pokémon.

entries.name: Wishing Piece
entries.flavorText:
Throw one into a Pokémon Den to attract
a wild Dynamax Pokémon.

entries.name: Wishing Star
entries.flavorText:
A stone found in the Galar region with a mysterious power.
It’s said that your dreams come true if you find one.

entries.name: Wood Mail
entries.shortEffectText: Lets a Trainer write a message and send it via Pokémon trade.
entries.effectText:
Used to send short messages to other players via Pokémon trading.  Trainer may compose a message from a finite list of words when giving this item to a Pokémon.  Once taken and read, a message may be erased and this item can be reused, or the message may be stored in the trainer’s PC.

Held
:   Holder cannot be placed in the PC.  Any move attempting to remove this item from the holder will fail.
entries.flavorText:
A piece of MAIL featuring a cute
SLAKOTH print.
It is to be held by a POKéMON.

entries.name: Wooden Crown
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious object made of wood. Part of it seems to
be chipped. Maybe it’s meant to be part of something.

entries.name: Works Key
entries.shortEffectText: Grants access to Valley Windworks.
entries.effectText: Opens the front door of the Valley Windworks.  Reusable.
entries.flavorText:
A large key for operating the doors of the
Valley Windworks in the canyon. It was held by
a Team Galactic Grunt.

entries.name: X Accuracy
entries.shortEffectText: Raises accuracy by one stage in battle.  Raises happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s accuracy by one stage.  Increases happiness by 1/1/0.
entries.flavorText:
An item that sharply boosts the accuracy of a
Pokémon during a battle. It wears off once the
Pokémon is withdrawn.

entries.name: X Accuracy 2
entries.shortEffectText: Raises accuracy by two stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s accuracy by two stages.
This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
It sharply raises the accuracy of a Pokémon in
battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

entries.name: X Accuracy 3
entries.shortEffectText: Raises accuracy by three stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s accuracy by three stages.
This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
It drastically raises the accuracy of a Pokémon in
battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn.

entries.name: X Accuracy 6
entries.shortEffectText: Raises accuracy by six stages in battle.  Wonder Launcher only.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Raises the target’s accuracy by six stages.

This item can only be obtained or used via the Wonder Launcher.
entries.flavorText:
It raises the accuracy of a Pokémon in battle
immensely. It wears off if the Pokémon
is withdrawn.

entries.name: Xtransceiver
entries.shortEffectText: Makes four-way video calls.
entries.effectText: Makes four-way video calls.  Used for plot advancement in-game, but also works with other players via the C-Gear.
entries.flavorText:
A high-tech transceiver with a camera function.
It allows up to four-way calls.

entries.name: Xtransceiver
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for xtranceiver--red
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for xtranceiver--red
entries.flavorText:
A high-tech transceiver with a camera function.
It allows up to four-way calls.

entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for xtranceiver--yellow
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for xtranceiver--yellow

It allows up to four-way calls.

entries.name: Yache Berry
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Consumed when struck by a super-effective Ice-type attack to halve the damage.
entries.effectText:
Held in battle
:   When the holder would take super-effective Ice-type damage, it consumes this item to halve the amount of damage taken.
entries.flavorText:
If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the
damage taken from one supereffective
Ice-type attack.

entries.name: Yellow Apricorn
entries.shortEffectText: Used to make a Moon Ball.
entries.effectText: May be given to Kurt in Azalea Town to produce a Moon Ball.
entries.flavorText: A yellow Apricorn. It has an invigorating scent.

entries.name: Yellow Flute
entries.shortEffectText: Cures confusion.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon in battle
:   Cures confusion.
entries.flavorText:
A flute made from yellow glass. It snaps Pokémon
out of confusion.

entries.name: Yellow Nectar
entries.shortEffectText: Changes Oricorio to Pom-Pom Style.
entries.effectText: Changes Oricorio to Pom-Pom Style.  Single-use and cannot be used in battle.
entries.flavorText:
A flower nectar obtained at Melemele Meadow.
It changes the form of certain species of Pokémon.

entries.name: Yellow Petal
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for yellow-petal
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for yellow-petal
entries.flavorText:
A pressed flower petal you receive from Sophocles
during Mina’s trial. The goal is to collect seven
different kinds of petals.

entries.name: Yellow Scarf
entries.shortEffectText: Raises the holder’s Toughness while in a contest.
entries.effectText: Held: Increases the holder’s Toughness during a Super Contest’s Visual Competition.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. During a contest,
it raises the Tough aspect of the Pokémon
holding it.

entries.name: Yellow Shard
entries.shortEffectText: No effect. Can be traded for items or moves.
entries.effectText:
No effect.

In Diamond and Pearl, trade ten for a Sandstorm TM in the house midway along the southern section of Route 212.

In Platinum, trade to move tutors on Route 212, in Snowpoint City, and in the Survival Area.  Eight shards total are required per tutelage, but the particular combination of colors varies by move.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for an Aspear Berry, a Iapapa Berry, and a Sitrus Berry with the Juggler near the Pokémon Center in Violet City.

In HeartGold and SoulSilver, trade one for a Grepa Berry, a Nomel Berry, and a Pinap Berry with the Juggler near the Pal Park entrance in Fuchsia City.
entries.flavorText:
A small yellow shard. It appears to be a fragment of
some sort of implement made long ago.

entries.name: Z-Power Ring
entries.shortEffectText: XXX new effect for z-power-ring
entries.effectText: XXX new effect for z-power-ring
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious ring that enables Pokémon to use
Z-Power. It requires both the willpower and the
physical power of the Trainer wearing it.

entries.name: Z-Ring
entries.shortEffectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to use Z-moves.
entries.effectText: Allows the player’s Pokémon to use Z-moves.
entries.flavorText:
A mysterious ring that enables Pokémon to use
Z-Power. It requires both the willpower and
the physical power of the Trainer wearing it.

entries.name: Zap Plate
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Electric-Type moves from holder do 20% more damage. Changes Arceus’s and Judgment’s type to Electric.
entries.effectText:
Held: Increases the power of the holder’s Electric moves by 20%.
Held by a Multitype Pokémon: Holder’s type becomes Electric.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. It’s a stone tablet
that boosts the power of Electric-type moves.

entries.name: Zapdos Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Zinc
entries.shortEffectText: Raises Special Defense and happiness.
entries.effectText:
Used on a party Pokémon
:   Increases Special Defense effort by 10, but won’t increase it beyond 100.  Increases happiness by 5/3/2.
entries.flavorText:
A nutritious drink for Pokémon. When consumed,
it raises the Sp. Def base points of a single Pokémon.

entries.name: Zoom Lens
entries.shortEffectText: Held: Provides a 1/5 (20%) boost in accuracy if the holder moves after the target.
entries.effectText:
Held: Raises the holder’s Accuracy by 20% when it goes last.
Ingame description is incorrect.
entries.flavorText:
An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder
moves after its target moves, its accuracy will
be boosted.

entries.name: Zubat Candy
entries.flavorText:
A candy that is packed with energy. When given to
certain Pokémon, it will increase all their stats at once.

entries.name: Zygarde Cube
entries.shortEffectText: Contains collected Zygarde cells/cores.  Can teach Zygarde moves.
entries.effectText: Contains collected Zygarde cells/cores.  Can teach Zygarde moves.
entries.flavorText:
An item in which Zygarde Cores and Cells are gathered.
You can also use it to change Zygarde’s forms.
